zh: 我回顾我在 2024 年加入的时候，收入是几亿美元，就在 2024 年底到 2025 年的轨迹，当我加入的 2025 年第二周，我们正在进入 2025 年的收入规划，我们有这些基础情况和激进情况的情景，而 Dario 在推行激进情况的情景，人们都在抓狂，说，“我们到底怎么才能达到那个目标？”

zh: 然后到了 2025 年底，好吧，大体数法则，基于你 100 亿的基准率，这里会有相当大的放缓，你要如何保持这种增长率？

en: The 19 billion number you quoted is from the end of Feb, so that is also out of date.

speaker: Unknown
en: And look, it's absolutely insane.

speaker: Unknown
en: The funniest thing is something that I've noticed internally is linear charts are just not cool.
zh: 最有趣的是我在内部注意到的一点是，线性图表一点都不酷。

speaker: Unknown
en: No one cares about linear charts.
zh: 没人在乎线性图表。

speaker: Unknown
en: Everything is log linear.
zh: 所有的东西都是对数线性的。

speaker: Unknown
en: You have to show me at log linear scale, and that's a scale we think.
zh: 你得给我看对数线性标度，那是我们要思考的标度。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think overall, we're just really hanging on by the seat of our pants.
zh: 而我想总的来说，我们真的只是在勉强维持。

speaker: Unknown
en: So we're trying to manage the growth and do the best that we can for our users.
zh: 所以我们正在试图管理增长，并为我们的用户尽我们所能。

speaker: Unknown
en: I was talking to somebody at Anthropic about you, and they said that basically anytime they want something to grow, they ask you to help and it works.
zh: 我和 Anthropic 的一个人谈论你，他们说基本上任何时候他们想要某个东西增长，他们请你帮忙，然后就奏效了。

speaker: Unknown
en: So you talked about just things are magical and amazing and Claude and all the tools you all built are amazing innately, and that's a big part of the reason they grow.
zh: 所以你刚才谈到，事情就是很神奇、很惊人，还有 Claude 和你们构建的所有工具都很棒，这是它们增长的一个很大原因。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think many people listening to this will be like, "What do you even do, Amol, with a magical microgod that just can do anything for you?
zh: 我想许多听众会想，“你到底在做什么，Amol，面对一个能为你做任何事的魔法微神？”

speaker: Unknown
en: Why do we need a growth person?
zh: 为什么我们需要一个增长负责人？

speaker: Unknown
en: What do you even do?"
zh: 你到底在做什么？”

speaker: Unknown
en: Talk about just the stuff that you focus on and maybe a couple of the wins that your team has shipped that has helped accelerate growth.
zh: 聊聊你关注的事情，也许还有你的团队发布的一些成果，这些成果帮助加速了增长。

speaker: Unknown
en: I would say they're not fully wrong.
zh: 我想说我并不完全认为他们错了。

speaker: Unknown
en: We're very, very lucky to have the best models in the world.
zh: 我们非常幸运拥有世界上最好的模型。

speaker: Unknown
en: We're very lucky to have products like Claude Code and Cowork.
zh: 我们非常幸运拥有像 Claude Code 和 Cowork 这样的产品。

speaker: Unknown
en: It certainly makes life a lot easier.
zh: 这确实让生活容易了很多。

speaker: Unknown
en: Having said that, I would say this is the hardest job I've had in my life, and that's having been a founder, having been an investment banker and other things like that, it's tough.
zh: 话虽如此，我想说这是我一生中最难的工作，考虑到我曾经做过创始人、投资银行家和其他类似的事情，这很难。

speaker: Unknown
en: And if I look at what do we do as a growth team here, I think ultimately it's the same categories of things that you would think about at a normal company.
zh: 如果我看看我们在增长团队做什么，我想最终这与你在普通公司会想到的同类事情是一样的。

speaker: Unknown
en: So we care about acquisition, how are we getting more people in the door, the intent of the people coming through the door.
zh: 所以我们关注获取，我们要如何让更多人进门，进门者的意图。

speaker: Unknown
en: We care about activation, the signup flow, funneling people to the right products, making them successful.
zh: 我们关注激活，注册流程，将用户引导到正确的产品，让他们成功。

speaker: Unknown
en: We care about things like monetization, free-to-paid conversion, pricing and packaging, all of that stuff that the categories of work is the same.
zh: 我们关注变现、免费转付费转化、定价和包装之类的事情，所有这些工作类别都是一样的。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think then probably the big differences is I would say that roughly 70% of what I spend my time on is what we internally refer to as success disasters.
zh: 然后我认为最大的区别可能在于，我大概 70% 的时间花在我们内部所谓的“成功的灾难”上。

speaker: Unknown
en: And that is where things have gone so well that other things are breaking now.
zh: 就是事情进行得太好了，导致其他东西现在坏了。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think anyone who's worked at companies that have gone through rapid growth, you think like Facebook, or Uber, or DoorDash early on, they understand this viscerally where scaling this much just brings a lot of challenges.
zh: 我想任何在经历过快速增长的公司工作过的人，想想 Facebook，或者 Uber，或者早期的 DoorDash，他们发自内心地理解这一点，这种规模的扩张带来了很多挑战。

speaker: Unknown
en: So if you think about each of those categories on acquisition, on activation, on monetization, there's just a ton of firefighting jumping from one urgent thing to another.
zh: 所以如果你想想获取、激活、变现的每一个类别，就有大量的救火工作，从一个紧急的事情跳到另一个。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it's often extremely painful.
zh: 这通常是非常痛苦的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it's funny because you look at all the charts, all the charts are green, fully up onto the right, and everyone's just like, it can be quite tough emotionally still.
zh: 这很有趣，因为你看到所有的图表，所有的图表都是绿色的，直线上升的，每个人都只是觉得，这可能在情感上还是相当艰难的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so you need to step back and just realize we're very lucky to have these problems.
zh: 所以你需要退后一步，意识到我们很幸运拥有这些问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: But that's 70% of my time, I'd say, is just these firefighting of success disasters.
zh: 但这就是我 70% 的时间，我想说，只是这些“成功的灾难”的救火工作。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think the 30% remaining is just much more standard bread and butter growth work where it's more proactive.
zh: 我想剩下的 30% 只是更标准的日常增长工作，那更具主动性。

speaker: Unknown
en: So you think about, okay, if we have limited resources, we have many different products, which of the products do we want to put some juice behind?
zh: 所以你想想，好吧，如果我们资源有限，我们有很多不同的产品，我们想把一些资源投入哪些产品？

speaker: Unknown
en: What does our long-term pricing and packaging look like, especially given that the technology's changing a lot and behavior and engagement trends are shifting.
zh: 我们的长期定价和包装是什么样的，特别是考虑到技术在快速变化，行为和参与趋势也在转移。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then things like we have a lot of new products coming up like, okay, you ship Cowork, now what?
zh: 然后像我们要推出很多新产品，好吧，你发布了 Cowork，现在怎么办？

speaker: Unknown
en: When is the right time that we should lean in as a growth team to start optimizing the core adoption funnel for Cowork?
zh: 作为增长团队，我们应该在什么时候开始优化 Cowork 的核心采用漏斗？

speaker: Unknown
en: So it's probably 70% just crazy firefighting, 30%, more bread and butter stuff.
zh: 所以大概 70% 是疯狂的救火，30% 是更日常的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay.
zh: 好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm going to dig into a lot of that stuff.
zh: 我要深入挖掘其中很多事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: One of the cleverest growth moves you all made recently was this idea of importing memory from ChatGPT where you just made it really easy and jumped on this trend of people getting really excited about Anthropic.
zh: 你们最近做的最聪明的增长举措之一就是这个从 ChatGPT 导入记忆的想法，你们让它变得非常容易，并抓住了人们对 Anthropic 感到兴奋的这个趋势。

speaker: Unknown
en: Is there anything you could share about the behind the scenes story of that feature?
zh: 关于这个功能的幕后故事，你有什么可以分享的吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: We're just thinking about what can we do to improve the cold start problem and improve the new user experience.
zh: 我们只是在思考我们能做什么来改善冷启动问题并改善新用户体验。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think that activation is a really big challenge in AI.
zh: 我认为激活在 AI 中是一个巨大的挑战。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so that's one example of something that we shipped that was very specific to a moment in time.
zh: 这就是我们发布的一个例子，它是非常针对特定时刻的。

speaker: Unknown
en: But ultimately that you zoom out, it's like, okay, how do you really make it easier for people who are signing up to have Claude understand who they are and understand how Claude can help them and get them to the right place?
zh: 但最终你拉远视角，就像，好吧，我们要如何真正让注册的人更容易让 Claude 理解他们是谁，理解 Claude 如何帮助他们，并让他们到达正确的地方？

speaker: Unknown
en: I want to follow that thread, activation.
zh: 我想顺着这个线索，激活。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's something that comes up a ton when I talk to people leading, driving growth on AI products.
zh: 这是当我与领导 AI 产品增长的人交谈时经常出现的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's just there are so much stuff trying to get your attention these days for people to get to a place where they, okay, wow, this is really going to be something I want to keep using.
zh: 现在有太多的东西在试图引起你的注意，人们很难到达一个地方，觉得，哇，这真的是我想继续使用的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: It proves to be really hard.
zh: 事实证明这真的很难。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it's also just unreliable sometimes, it's not going to be magical.
zh: 而且它有时也只是不可靠的，它不会是魔法般的。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's AI, it's non-deterministic.
zh: 它是 AI，它是非确定性的。

speaker: Unknown
en: I guess, one, is just how important is focusing on activation, getting people to that aha moment with AI products?
zh: 我想，第一，专注于激活有多重要，让人们使用 AI 产品达到那个顿悟时刻？

speaker: Unknown
en: And two, what are some things you've learned about how to do that well with Claude or AI in general?
zh: 第二，关于如何通过 Claude 或 AI 做到这一点，你学到了些什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, it's a good question.
zh: 是的，这是个好问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think that activation, it's critical and defining that as early activation, call it day zero, day one product experience.
zh: 我认为激活是关键，将其定义为早期激活，比如第零天、第一天的产品体验。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think that historically anyone who's been in growth or been in product understands that that's usually one of the highest levers that you have to actually even increase longer term retention.
zh: 我认为历史上任何从事过增长或产品工作的人都知道，这通常是你提高长期留存率的最有力杠杆之一。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think that the importance of that has just gotten exponentially higher.
zh: 我认为这种重要性已经呈指数级增长。

speaker: Unknown
en: Now, zooming out, I feel like one of the biggest problems in the industry is capability overhang where the models are just getting better so quickly.
zh: 现在，拉远视角，我觉得行业最大的问题之一是能力过剩，模型变得越来越好，快得惊人。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the real challenge is on the product side of how do we start to diffuse those benefits to people where even internally, there's new models coming internally and you're sitting there, you're so busy.
zh: 真正的挑战在于产品方面，我们要如何开始将这些利益扩散给人们，甚至内部都有新模型发布，你坐在那里，你是如此忙碌。

speaker: Unknown
en: And when a new model's available, you need to really carve out time to be like, "What can this do?
zh: 当有一个新模型可用时，你需要真正抽出时间来说，“这能做什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: How do I need to update my priors?"
zh: 我需要如何更新我的先验看法？”

speaker: Unknown
en: And if you think about more broadly for most people, you may have a model that is like, you may have AGI or some model that can do all sorts of crazy things, but if people's instinct is to come there and be like, "Hey, what's the weather in SF?"
zh: 如果你更广泛地考虑对大多数人来说，你可能有一个模型，你可能拥有 AGI 或某种可以做各种疯狂事情的模型，但如果人们的本能是来到那里并说，“嘿，SF 的天气怎么样？”

speaker: Unknown
en: Then they're not going to get the most out of the product.
zh: 那么他们就无法从产品中获得最大收益。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so I think that it's tough because the model capabilities are rising so much.
zh: 所以我认为这很艰难，因为模型能力提升得如此之多。

speaker: Unknown
en: So if I think about, okay, back when we had, I don't know, I want to say Opus 4, there's a series of things the model can do at that point, and Opus 4.5 unlocked a whole bunch of new things.
zh: 所以如果我认为，好吧，回到我们拥有 Opus 4 的时候，我想说，在那时模型可以做一系列事情，而 Opus 4.5 解锁了一整套新东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: You think about, okay, we sit there, we've got this new model, Opus 4.
zh: 你想一下，好吧，我们坐在那里，我们有了这个新模型，Opus 4。

speaker: Unknown
en: The time to then go and run a bunch of tests, figure out, okay, there's the capabilities from this new model, what are the right on ramps to guide people to those features?
zh: 然后去进行一系列测试的时间，弄清楚，好吧，这个新模型有这些能力，引导人们使用这些功能的正确入口是什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: You run tests, you get the learnings, you then ship a new flow.
zh: 你运行测试，你获得经验教训，然后你发布一个新的流程。

speaker: Unknown
en: By then, you may already have the next model, which unlocks newer capabilities that makes all these learnings irrelevant.
zh: 到那时，你可能已经有了下一个模型，它解锁了更新的能力，使得所有这些经验教训变得无关紧要。

speaker: Unknown
en: So it's actually just a really difficult problem to stay on top of.
zh: 所以这实际上是一个非常难以应对的问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think that many of the same things, same old trends in growth and activation remain, I think, accurate where to me it's like ultimately some of the highest leverages from finding the right product or the right feature for the right user.
zh: 我认为许多相同的事情，在增长和激活方面的相同旧趋势，我认为，仍然是准确的，对我来说，这就像最终找到最高杠杆率的方法就是为合适的用户找到合适的产品或合适的功能。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think that one learning, seeing this time and time again across companies, actually the right friction helps and adding more friction usually works if you do it the right way.
zh: 我认为有一个经验教训，在公司中一次又一次地看到这一点，实际上正确的摩擦会有所帮助，如果你以正确的方式添加更多的摩擦通常会奏效。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think that's something I've consistently seen that we've seen here as well.
zh: 我认为这是我一直看到的事情，我们在这里也看到了。

speaker: Unknown
en: So to me, I think it's really being able to identify what are the characteristics of a user that allows you to then recommend them to the right feature or product and not being shy about adding friction to do that, I think is probably the single biggest thing that's important here.
zh: 所以对我来说，我认为实际上是能够识别出允许你将他们推荐给合适的功能或产品的用户的特征，并且不羞于为此添加摩擦，我认为这可能是这里最重要的一件事。

speaker: Unknown
en: When I asked Ben Mann, one of the co-founders, former podcast guest, what to ask you about, and this is what he highlighted, is your experience, especially at Mercury redoing onboarding and making it magical.
zh: 当我问 Ben Mann，一位联合创始人，以前的播客嘉宾，问你什么问题时，这就是他强调的，是你的经验，特别是在 Mercury 重新设计入职引导并使其变得神奇。

speaker: Unknown
en: And basically he's in the same place as you of just how important it is for people to understand what the AI tool is capable of to help people decide to use it and stick with it.
zh: 基本上他和你在同一个立场，即人们理解 AI 工具能做什么是多么重要，以帮助人们决定使用它并坚持使用它。

speaker: Unknown
en: Is there an example of something you changed in onboarding that helped significantly improve activation?
zh: 有没有你在入职引导中改变的例子，它显著提高了激活率？

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, it's a great question.
zh: 是的，这是一个很好的问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I like that it brings up Mercury.
zh: 我喜欢它提到了 Mercury。

speaker: Unknown
en: I talk about their product a lot.
zh: 我经常谈论他们的产品。

speaker: Unknown
en: So worked on the growth team at Mercury, I think it's a fantastic product.
zh: 所以在 Mercury 的增长团队工作，我认为这是一个很棒的产品。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's something many people use, and the reason they use it is because the better banking experience than its-
zh: 很多人使用它，他们使用它的原因是因为它比它的银行业务体验更好

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, I'm a very happy customer just to put that out there.
zh: 是的，为了说明这一点，我就是一个非常满意的客户。

speaker: Unknown
en: I love it.
zh: 我很喜欢它。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's a great product.
zh: 这是一个很棒的产品。

speaker: Unknown
en: Highly recommend it.
zh: 强烈推荐它。

speaker: Unknown
en: They have personal banking.
zh: 他们有个人银行业务。

speaker: Unknown
en: Everyone should go and use it.
zh: 大家都应该去使用它。

speaker: Unknown
en: Right.
zh: 对的。

speaker: Unknown
en: They just launched that.
zh: 他们刚刚推出了那个。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so I think that the interesting thing about Mercury is the core value is that it's a better experience.
zh: 所以我认为关于 Mercury 的有趣之处在于核心价值是它是一种更好的体验。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's the reason you use it.
zh: 这就是你使用它的原因。

speaker: Unknown
en: You're not getting better other things, just it's a better product experience.
zh: 你没有获得其他更好的东西，只是它是一个更好的产品体验。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so that ethos is very, very deeply held within the company.
zh: 所以这种精神在公司内部被非常、非常深刻地坚持着。

speaker: Unknown
en: It comes from a number of the founders.
zh: 它来自许多创始人。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think that we had a big push one quarter when I was there on the onboarding flow.
zh: 我认为我们在那里的时候，有一个季度我们在入职引导流程上做出了巨大的推动。

speaker: Unknown
en: So onboarding flows for banking institutions and regulated entities are extremely complex.
zh: 所以对于银行机构和受监管实体来说，入职引导流程极其复杂。

speaker: Unknown
en: The amount of time I've spent on the difference between a registered agent address and a legal address and a physical address, these things are very, very complex.
zh: 我花了太多时间在注册代理人地址和法定地址以及物理地址之间的区别上，这些事情非常、非常复杂。

speaker: Unknown
en: And we basically looked at the onboarding flow and we were like, okay, we've invested so much in quality and the rest of the product, but we haven't really done it here.
zh: 我们基本上看了入职引导流程，我们说，好吧，我们在质量和产品的其余部分投入了这么多，但我们还没有真正在这里做。

speaker: Unknown
en: And this is the first experience that people have.
zh: 这是人们的第一次体验。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so we said, forget metrics, forget growth, forget everything else.
zh: 所以我们说，忘掉指标，忘掉增长，忘掉其他一切。

speaker: Unknown
en: As the growth team on conversion, we're going to spend a whole quarter fixing quality in this flow.
zh: 作为负责转化的增长团队，我们要花整整一个季度修复这个流程中的质量。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so that's all we did.
zh: 所以我们就做了这些。

speaker: Unknown
en: Forget the metrics.
zh: 忘掉指标。

speaker: Unknown
en: We're just going to make this as good of an experience as we can and fix all these.
zh: 我们只是要使其尽可能好的体验并修复所有这些。

en: You go back from one field to the other field and adding in your beneficial ownership details.
zh: 你从一个字段回到另一个字段并添加你的受益所有权详细信息。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it actually ended up being, and probably until I joined here, the single most impactful quarter that I've ever had as a growth PM in terms of the impact that it had.
zh: 它实际上最终成为，可能直到我加入这里，我曾经作为增长 PM 拥有过的最有影响力的季度，就其产生的影响而言。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so we saw a significant uplift on basically our onboarding started to completion from just focusing on quality.
zh: 所以我们看到了显著的提升，基本上我们的入职引导开始到完成仅仅通过专注于质量。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so that to me is a broader learning around quality drives growth that I think I've tried to bring to Anthropic.
zh: 所以对我来说，这是一个关于质量驱动增长的更广泛的经验教训，我认为我一直试图将其带到 Anthropic。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think for us at Anthropic, some of the things that we've done in the onboarding flow is basically we ask users questions around who they are, what their interest areas are.
zh: 我觉得对于我们在 Anthropic 来说，我们在入门流程中做的一些事情基本上是围绕用户是谁、他们的兴趣领域是什么来询问用户问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: And we then use that to recommend different products and features.
zh: 然后我们利用这些信息来推荐不同的产品和功能。

speaker: Unknown
en: And number of people look at the flow and they're like, "You have so much friction.
zh: 很多人看这个流程，他们会说，“你有这么多摩擦。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's such a long flow."
zh: 这是一个这么长的流程。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And I'm like, "We have the data.
zh: 我会说，“我们有数据。

speaker: Unknown
en: We're kind of happy with how that's performing."
zh: 我们对它的表现还算满意。”

speaker: Unknown
en: What is your kind of philosophy on good friction versus bad friction?
zh: 你对良性摩擦与不良摩擦的理念是什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: I've just seen time and time again at every job I've been in growth that adding friction and adding the right steps leads to higher conversion and higher funnel completion.
zh: 我在从事增长的每一份工作中一次又一次地看到，增加摩擦并增加正确的步骤会带来更高的转化率和更高的漏斗完成率。

speaker: Unknown
en: So you want to get rid of... And especially if you have high volume, you should test the majority of this and just learn and see, does this apply to your business as well?
zh: 所以你想摆脱……特别是如果你有高流量，你应该对其中大部分进行测试，然后学习并观察，这是否也适用于你的业务？

speaker: Unknown
en: But you want to get rid of annoying friction that doesn't add value.
zh: 但你想摆脱那些不增加价值的、恼人的摩擦。

speaker: Unknown
en: But I think the most simple understanding people have is just solve time for value, cut all the steps and just get them into the product.
zh: 但我认为人们最简单的理解只是为了价值解决时间，砍掉所有步骤，直接让他们进入产品。

speaker: Unknown
en: And that doesn't work most times.
zh: 但这在大多数时候并不奏效。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think if you've really thoroughly tested your flows, I look at the companies I've been at, MasterClass.
zh: 我认为如果你真正彻底测试了你的流程，我看看我在过的那些公司，MasterClass。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you go through MasterClass's purchase flow right now, you'll go through all these steps in this quiz when you land on, when you're trying to buy.
zh: 如果你现在走一遍 MasterClass 的购买流程，当你着陆、当你试图购买时，你会通过这个测验中的所有这些步骤。

speaker: Unknown
en: And you're like, "I came here to buy and it's taking me through all these questions, what are you here for, et cetera."
zh: 然后你会想，“我是来买东西的，它却带我通过所有这些问题，你来这里是做什么等等。”

speaker: Unknown
en: I think it's easy to look at that and be like, "Why do they have this?
zh: 我认为很容易看着那个然后说，“他们为什么要弄这个？

speaker: Unknown
en: This is a terrible thing.
zh: 这太糟糕了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Just cut it all."
zh: 把它全部砍掉吧。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And it's like, no, that's been thoroughly tested.
zh: 然后实际上，不，这已经经过了彻底的测试。

speaker: Unknown
en: And actually that was a significant revenue driver because it helps users feel that the product is for them by understanding what their interests are and then recommending the content and classes there.
zh: 实际上这是一个重要的收入驱动因素，因为它通过了解用户的兴趣是什么，然后推荐那里的内容和课程，来帮助用户感觉这个产品是为他们准备的。

speaker: Unknown
en: One of the growth PMs on our team left to join Calm, Calm.com, the meditation app.
zh: 我们团队中的一位增长 PM 离职去加入了 Calm，Calm.com，那个冥想应用。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you go to Calm's, their landing page and you go through their purchase flow, their login flow, you'll see a quiz.
zh: 如果你去 Calm 的落地页，你走一遍他们的购买流程，他们的登录流程，你会看到一个测验。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's not a coincidence that at Mercury we also tested, I think Immad posted on Twitter, we broke out some steps in onboarding.
zh: 这并非巧合，在 Mercury 我们也测试了，我想 Immad 在 Twitter 上发过，我们在入门流程中分解了一些步骤。

speaker: Unknown
en: And just having one screen, if you have five or six different form inputs and you often break that into two screens and reduce the cognitive load to people, that is something that performs well.
zh: 只有一个屏幕，如果你有五个或六个不同的表单输入，你经常把它分解成两个屏幕，减少人们的认知负荷，这就是一种表现良好的做法。

speaker: Unknown
en: We added steps into the flow there that actually performed well, same at Anthropic.
zh: 我们在那里向流程中添加了实际上表现良好的步骤，在 Anthropic 也是如此。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I think that the takeaway to me is cut friction when it doesn't add to the experience of helping a user understand why the product is for them.
zh: 所以我认为对我来说，结论是当摩擦无助于帮助用户理解为什么产品是为他们准备的体验时，就削减摩擦。

speaker: Unknown
en: But if you can help users understand a product, why product is for them and how to use it and what's most relevant to them, and it's going to add friction, don't shy away from it.
zh: 但如果你能帮助用户理解产品，为什么产品是为他们准备的，以及如何使用它，什么对他们最相关，虽然这会增加摩擦，不要回避它。

speaker: Unknown
en: Test it, confirm that it works for you, but I think this is something that most growth practitioners deeply understand.
zh: 测试它，确认它对你有效，但我认为这是大多数增长从业者深刻理解的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: And for them is really important there, what you described is adding friction to better understand who they are so that you know how to recommend the right thing for them.
zh: 对他们来说那里真的很重要，你所描述的是增加摩擦以更好地了解他们是谁，这样你就知道如何为他们推荐正确的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: Correct.
zh: 正确。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yes.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And that done right, it just flows through.
zh: 而且做得正确的话，它就会顺畅流动。

speaker: Unknown
en: So it helps you with activation, but then it helps you with lifecycle.
zh: 所以它帮助你激活，然后它帮助你生命周期。

speaker: Unknown
en: You know more about those users and why they're here.
zh: 你更了解那些用户以及他们为什么在这里。

speaker: Unknown
en: And most sophisticated businesses, you can then, even if someone drops off, you can do lookalike targeting and you can get them at the ad layer as well.
zh: 对于大多数复杂的业务，你甚至可以在用户流失的情况下进行相似定位，你也可以在广告层获取他们。

speaker: Unknown
en: So that initial piece of how you understand who the user is just like it's a juice that just keeps on giving if you then use it right for the down funnel.
zh: 所以那部分最初的你如何了解用户是谁的信息就像是一种果汁，如果你然后在漏斗下游正确使用它，它就会不断给予回报。

speaker: Unknown
en: Everyone's about to go do a bunch of tear downs of Claude's onboarding, MasterClass onboarding, Mercury onboarding.
zh: 大家都准备去对 Claude 的入门流程、MasterClass 的入门流程、Mercury 的入门流程做一堆拆解。

speaker: Unknown
en: As a tangent, I was at this PM dinner recently and I was asking all the PMs, how has your role as a PM changed most with AI?
zh: 作为题外话，我最近在这个 PM 晚餐会上，我问所有的 PM，作为 PM 你的角色在哪些方面因为 AI 而改变最多？

speaker: Unknown
en: Where has AI most impacted what you do?
zh: AI 在哪些方面最大地影响了你在做的事情？

speaker: Unknown
en: And one of the PM's answer that is actually doing competitive analysis, doing a bunch of teardowns of what other people are doing for pricing pages and onboarding.
zh: 其中一个 PM 的回答实际上是做竞品分析，做一堆关于其他人定价页面和入门流程的拆解。

speaker: Unknown
en: So it's easy to do now.
zh: 所以现在这很容易做。

speaker: Unknown
en: Just, "Hey, Cowork..." I don't know, would you use Cowork for this or Claude?
zh: 只需要，“嘿，Cowork……”我不知道，你会用 Cowork 做这个还是 Claude？

speaker: Unknown
en: What would you use for that?
zh: 你会用什么来做那个？

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay, this is good.
zh: 好的，这很好。

speaker: Unknown
en: Help people pick which tool to use.
zh: 帮助人们选择使用哪个工具。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you want to go do a bunch of teardowns of other competitors' onboarding flows, what would you use?
zh: 如果你想去对其他竞争对手的入门流程做一堆拆解，你会用什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: You can use Cowork for this, right?
zh: 你可以用 Cowork 做这个，对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: So you have Cowork with the Chrome extension.
zh: 所以你有带 Chrome 扩展的 Cowork。

speaker: Unknown
en: So if you task Cowork with a Chrome extension, go and look for these flows and give me a view of what's working, what's not.
zh: 所以如果你给带 Chrome 扩展的 Cowork 派个任务，去寻找这些流程并给我一个关于什么有效、什么无效的视图。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's definitely something that Cowork can do.
zh: 这绝对是 Cowork 可以做的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: Cool.
zh: 酷。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I imagine that's one of the challenges is you have all these tools now, just which ones for me.
zh: 我想挑战之一在于，你现在有所有这些工具，究竟是哪些适合我。

speaker: Unknown
en: By the way, the Chrome extension, I use it all the time.
zh: 顺便说一句，那个 Chrome 扩展，我一直在用。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's amazing.
zh: 它很棒。

speaker: Unknown
en: I want to drill a little bit further into the growth org.
zh: 我想更深入地挖掘一下增长组织。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's this whole meme on Twitter the other day of just like you have one growth marketer driving all growth at Anthropic and it's like, okay, that's crazy.
zh: 前几天 Twitter 上有个梗，就像你有一个增长营销人员驱动着 Anthropic 的所有增长，这就像，好吧，太疯狂了。

speaker: Unknown
en: How many growth people are there?
zh: 有多少增长人员？

speaker: Unknown
en: What's the rough org structure of the growth team?
zh: 增长团队的大致组织结构是怎样的？

speaker: Unknown
en: We're roughly maybe 40 people now.
zh: 我们现在大概有 40 人。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so we are, I think, structured very much like a traditional growth team in that we have horizontals of growth platform and monetization who think about the sphere of growth across the entirety of our products.
zh: 所以我认为，我们的结构非常像一个传统的增长团队，因为我们有增长平台和变现的横向团队，他们思考跨越我们所有产品的增长领域。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then we have more audience-focused growth pods.
zh: 然后我们有更多以受众为中心的增长小组。

speaker: Unknown
en: You can think about B2B growth, you can think about Claude Code growth, knowledge worker growth, API growth.
zh: 你可以想到 B2B 增长，你可以想到 Claude Code 增长，知识工作者增长，API 增长。

speaker: Unknown
en: So really these audiences to keep a narrow focus, which is the thing you have to do when you have so many different products.
zh: 所以实际上是这些受众保持着狭窄的关注点，这是当你有这么多不同产品时必须做的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then these horizontals that think about things across the board.
zh: 然后是这些横向团队，他们全面思考问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: And is it across a team of engineers, designers, PMs?
zh: 这是一个由工程师、设计师、产品经理组成的团队吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: What's the functions within this growth org?
zh: 这个增长组织内部有哪些职能？

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, it's engineers, designers, PMs, data.
zh: 是的，有工程师、设计师、产品经理、数据。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think that overall the shape of the org is quite similar to, I'd say a traditional growth team.
zh: 我认为总的来说，组织的形状与传统的增长团队非常相似。

speaker: Unknown
en: Probably the things that are maybe different is that I think that we index a lot more towards larger swings as opposed to smaller optimizations.
zh: 可能不同的是，我认为我们要比小型优化更倾向于大的尝试。

speaker: Unknown
en: If I think about a traditional growth team, I would've probably done maybe 60%, 70% of my time on small to medium bets, 20% to 30% on larger swings.
zh: 如果我想象一个传统的增长团队，我可能把 60%、70% 的时间花在小到中型的赌注上，20% 到 30% 花在大的尝试上。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think that for us, we flip it a lot.
zh: 我认为对我们来说，我们很大程度上颠倒了这一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: We do much more the other way where it's 70-30 or more like 50-50 rather than indexing towards smaller bets.
zh: 我们更多地采用另一种方式，即 70-30 或更像是 50-50，而不是倾向于小型赌注。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's probably one of the biggest changes, I think.
zh: 我认为这可能是最大的变化之一。

speaker: Unknown
en: Just to highlight that, what's interesting there is at the scale you guys are at, a 1% win is massive in the scheme of things.
zh: 强调一下，有趣的是在你们这样的规模下，1% 的收益在整体局势中是巨大的。

speaker: Unknown
en: So it's interesting that even at the scale you're at, you're not focusing on these micro optimizations.
zh: 所以有趣的是，即使在你们这样的规模下，你们也不关注这些微优化。

speaker: Unknown
en: It is easy.
zh: 这很容易。

speaker: Unknown
en: You could easily focus on these small optimizations and then you tally them up at the end of the quarter and you're like, "Look how much impact we made."
zh: 你很容易关注这些小的优化，然后在季度末把它们加起来，你会说，“看看我们产生了多大的影响。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And you could do that.
zh: 你可以那样做。

speaker: Unknown
en: Another billion.
zh: 又是十亿。

speaker: Unknown
en: No big deal.
zh: 没什么大不了的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: But the thing is we've been tracking at 10X year-on-year, and that's the thing that we keep in mind.
zh: 但问题是我们一直在追踪 10 倍的同比增长，这就是我们要记住的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think it ultimately comes down to our fixation of this company about the exponential.
zh: 我认为这最终归结为我们公司对指数级的执念。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think if you look at anyone who's talking from Anthropic about basically anything, we always talk about the exponential.
zh: 我认为如果你看看任何从 Anthropic 谈论基本任何事情的人，我们总是谈论指数级。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's effectively as model capabilities continue to grow on an exponential and the tools around them enable a better job of diffusing that into useful use cases, you basically just keep unlocking new markets where the value of those markets significantly dwarfs what the value of the previous markets were.
zh: 实际上，随着模型能力继续呈指数级增长，以及围绕它们的工具能够更好地将其扩散到有用的用例中，你基本上只是不断解锁新市场，这些市场的价值明显超过了以前市场的价值。

speaker: Unknown
en: And agentic coding's a great example.
zh: 智能体编码就是一个很好的例子。

speaker: Unknown
en: It didn't exist a year, a year and a half ago.
zh: 它在一年、一年半前还不存在。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then now just the value of agentic coding is bigger than the previous market of AI coding use.
zh: 而现在仅仅是智能体编码的价值就比以前的 AI 编码使用市场要大。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so I think that is the core thing here where just that the future product value is in order of magnitude higher than it is today.
zh: 所以我认为这是核心所在，即未来的产品价值比今天高一个数量级。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think about, I don't know, a normal business, number of companies that I maybe mentioned, or you think about a trading app or a grocery delivery product, many of the leading companies, they're great businesses.
zh: 我想到，我不知道，一个正常的业务，我可能提到的几家公司，或者你想像一个交易应用程序或杂货配送产品，许多领先的公司，它们是伟大的业务。

speaker: Unknown
en: But if I think about what is the product value that a company, like a standard, call it a grocery delivery app, what is the product value you get as an end user today versus two years from now?
zh: 但如果我想想一个公司的产品价值是什么，比如一个标准的，称之为杂货配送应用程序，作为最终用户，你今天获得的产品价值与两年后相比是什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: I look at it as like, okay, in two years from now, even if you're shipping all these new products, as an end user, the value you get-
zh: 我把它看作，好吧，在两年后，即使你发布了所有这些新产品，作为最终用户，你获得的价值——

speaker: Unknown
en: You're shipping all these new products.
zh: 你们发布了所有这些新产品。

speaker: Unknown
en: As an end user, the value you get from that product maybe goes up 30 to 50% if the company's done a really good job of shipping new features.
zh: 作为最终用户，你从该产品获得的价值可能只会增长 30% 到 50%，如果公司在发布新功能方面做得非常好的话。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's not exponential though.
zh: 但这不是指数级的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so if I think about, "Okay, you're here today.
zh: 所以如果我想想，“好吧，你今天在这里。

speaker: Unknown
en: In two years, you're going to have 30 to 50% more product value."
zh: 在两年后，你将拥有 30% 到 50% 的产品价值。”

speaker: Unknown
en: Then as a growth team, the relative differential of the product value in two years from now relative to today, I can actually capture a decent percentage of that with the small to medium optimizations that typically have higher conviction as opposed to larger bets.
zh: 那么作为一个增长团队，两年后的产品价值相对于今天的相对差异，我实际上可以通过小到中型的优化来捕获相当比例的价值，这些优化通常具有更高的把握度，而不是大的赌注。

speaker: Unknown
en: But for Anthropic, it's not really that way, where because of the exponential and our products being very, very, very ... The product value coming from AI, the product value that we will deliver in two years' time is probably a 1,000x, a 100 to a 1,000x what it is today.
zh: 但对于 Anthropic 来说，情况并非如此，因为指数级以及我们的产品非常、非常、非常……来自 AI 的产品价值，我们将在两年后交付的产品价值可能是今天的 1,000 倍，100 到 1,000 倍。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so if I think about that, and it's like there's so much value on offer, you need to shift more towards, "Okay, we need to take larger bets and we need to not miss the forest for the trees."
zh: 所以如果考虑到这一点，就像有如此多的价值提供，你需要更多地转向，“好吧，我们需要采取更大的赌注，我们需要不要只见树木不见森林。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And so that's why we still do all the smaller optimizations.
zh: 这就是为什么我们仍然做所有这些较小的优化。

speaker: Unknown
en: It really matters, and no one else is going to do some of these things and so we need to do it.
zh: 这真的很重要，而且没有其他人会做其中的一些事情，所以我们需要做。

speaker: Unknown
en: The compounding value is not immaterial, but we do take on much larger core productey type of swings as well.
zh: 这种复利价值并非微不足道，但我们确实也会承担更大的、更核心产品类型的大动作。

speaker: Unknown
en: So you mentioned the Chrome Extension.
zh: 所以你提到了 Chrome 扩展程序。

speaker: Unknown
en: That is now the thing that underpins a number of use cases on Cowork and Claude Code as well.
zh: 现在这个东西支撑了 Cowork 和 Claude Code 上的多个用例。

speaker: Unknown
en: And that's something that the growth team built.
zh: 这是增长团队构建的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's a very AI-peeled product that is a very research-heavy product that we were just bullish on it.
zh: 这是一个非常有 AI 氛围的产品，也是一个研究成分很重的产品，我们就是看好它。

speaker: Unknown
en: We had an engineer who was very bullish on it and we were just like, "Hey, no one else is doing it.
zh: 我们有一位非常看好它的工程师，我们就像说，「嘿，没别人在做这个。

speaker: Unknown
en: We're going to do it."
zh: 我们要做。」

speaker: Unknown
en: And so that's the sort of thing that I wouldn't have done at another company.
zh: 所以这就是我在其他公司不会做的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: Oh, wow.
zh: 哦，哇。

speaker: Unknown
en: I did not know that.
zh: 我不知道那一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: So one takeaway here is there's stuff to extract from your advice that is only true in Anthropic and then there's, what can other companies learn from this experience that you've had?
zh: 所以这里的一个结论是，从你的建议中可以提取出一些只在 Anthropic 成立的东西，然后也有，其他公司可以从你的这段经历中学到什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: So one is, is your sense that if you're working in AI, shift more of the pie chart towards bigger bets because in the future, the opportunity is so large, you want to find those as soon as possible versus micro-optimize?
zh: 所以一个是，你的感觉是，如果你在 AI 领域工作，要把饼图中更多的部分转移到更大的赌注上，因为未来的机会如此巨大，你想尽快找到它们，而不是进行微优化？

speaker: Unknown
en: To be more specific there, I would say that if the primary value that your product delivers is underpinned by AI as a central element of it, then I think you should operate this way.
zh: 具体来说，我会说，如果你的产品提供的核心价值是以 AI 为核心要素支撑的，那么我认为你应该以这种方式运作。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I don't know, companies like Lovable, Cursor, all these great businesses that are, as the exponential rises, their value props are also going to continue to rise significantly.
zh: 所以我不知道，像 Lovable、Cursor 这样的公司，所有这些很棒的企业，随着指数级上升，它们的价值主张也将继续显著上升。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you're building a product where it's an AI-first product, then I would definitely operate in this way.
zh: 如果你正在构建一个 AI优先的产品，那么我肯定会以这种方式运作。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think if you're building a product where it's not necessarily an AI- first product and you have some AI features that are on the side, but it's not the core of your value, I don't know that I would operate this way.
zh: 我认为如果你正在构建的产品不一定是 AI优先的产品，并且你有一些 AI 功能在旁边，但这不是你价值的核心，我不确定我会以这种方式运作。

speaker: Unknown
en: It would depend on how is the rest of the product org staffed and how is the growth org staffed in relation to that.
zh: 这取决于产品组织的其余部分是如何配备的，以及增长组织与之相比是如何配备的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay, awesome.
zh: 好的，太棒了。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then in terms of the way you are structured, I thought that was really interesting.
zh: 然后就你们的组织方式而言，我觉得真的很有趣。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's a combination of different sorts of things.
zh: 它是不同种类事物的组合。

speaker: Unknown
en: So there's the API growth, there's Claude Code growth, but then there's also personas, like a vertical of knowledge workers and B2B.
zh: 所以有 API 增长，有 Claude Code 增长，但也有 personas（人设），比如知识工作者和 B2B 的垂直领域。

speaker: Unknown
en: Is that intentional, some specific bets and one just broad market opportunity?
zh: 这是有意的，是一些具体的赌注，还是仅仅是一个广泛的市场机会？

speaker: Unknown
en: When you have one product, it's easier to have a growth team that's more purely on the funnel.
zh: 当你有一个产品时，拥有一个更纯粹专注于漏斗的增长团队会更容易。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's like you have the conversion, you have activation, you have monetization.
zh: 就像你有转化，你有激活，你有变现。

speaker: Unknown
en: But as you start to get into having multiple products, I think that's harder because if you do that, then if you just have one activation team, for example, but then you have Claude Code, you have Cowork, you have all the other things.
zh: 但随着你开始拥有多个产品，我认为这就更难了，因为如果你那样做，例如你只有一个激活团队，但你有 Claude Code，你有 Cowork，你有所有其他东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: They're very different audiences and they're very different sets of cross-functional stakeholders internally.
zh: 它们是非常不同的受众，并且在内部有非常不同的跨职能利益相关者群体。

speaker: Unknown
en: So we're kind of looking at, what is the thing?
zh: 所以我们正在看，那件事是什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: And all org structures are not perfect and they're right for a point in time, but we're looking for what is the structure that allows us to have as much focus.
zh: 所有的组织结构都不完美，它们在特定的时间点是正确的，但我们在寻找能够让我们拥有尽可能多专注的结构。

speaker: Unknown
en: Focus is a really big thing, and on audience and problems.
zh: 专注是一件非常重要的事情，专注于受众和问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: And also, the tie-ins to cross-functional partners is really, really important.
zh: 此外，与跨职能合作伙伴的联系也真的非常重要。

speaker: Unknown
en: The folks on our Claude Code growth team, they work extremely closely with Cat and Boris and the others on that team.
zh: 我们在 Claude Code 增长团队的人，他们与 Cat 和 Boris 以及该团队的其他人极其紧密地合作。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so that tie-in is really important as well.
zh: 所以这种联系也真的很重要。

speaker: Unknown
en: So you've done growth at a lot of different companies, a lot of basically, let's call it traditional growth before Anthropic.
zh: 所以你在很多不同的公司都做过增长，基本上就是，让我们称之为在 Anthropic 之前的传统增长。

speaker: Unknown
en: How else is growth as a function and as a skillset changing with the rise of AI, AI products, and AI startups?
zh: 随着 AI、AI 产品和 AI 初创公司的兴起，增长作为一个职能和技能组合还在发生哪些变化？

speaker: Unknown
en: I think if your core product value is very backed by AI, then it is shifting where you're skewing more towards larger bets as opposed to smaller, medium experiments.
zh: 我认为如果你的核心产品价值非常依赖于 AI，那么它正在转变，你更多地倾向于更大的赌注，而不是中小型实验。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think in other things, it may be a big thing related to this that I think will accelerate this, which I am really interested to see how it will play out, is we are starting to look at how do we automate growth, which I think is a really interesting area.
zh: 我认为在其他事情中，与此相关的一件我认为将加速这一点的大事，我真的很有兴趣看看它会如何发展，就是我们正在开始研究如何自动化增长，我认为这是一个非常有趣的领域。

speaker: Unknown
en: So our growth platform team, we're very lucky we have Alexey Komissarouk who teaches growth engineering at Reforge, and he's just the guy on the team.
zh: 所以我们的增长平台团队，我们非常幸运我们有 Alexey Komissarouk，他在 Reforge 教授增长工程，他就是团队里的那个人。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so he is driving this effort.

en: The name is, it's a little cringey.

speaker: Unknown

speaker: Unknown

speaker: Unknown
en: I did not come up with that, but really it's an effort to look at how can we use Claude to automate growth experimentation?
zh: 不是我想出来的，但实际上这是在研究我们如何使用 Claude 来自动化增长实验？

speaker: Unknown
en: And it is still very, very small.
zh: 它仍然非常非常小。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's still very, very early.

speaker: Unknown
en: We kicked it off only, I think, a couple of months ago.
zh: 我们只是启动了它，我想，几个月前。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think before Opus 4.5, it wasn't really possible.

speaker: Unknown
en: We were just not seeing the results.

speaker: Unknown

speaker: Unknown
en: And so this, I think, it will happen more and more across the industry, where basically if you think about, okay ... I think this can happen all across product, but growth teams in particular because there's this whole body of work that is very small optimizations, I think are just more inherently suited to tackle this earlier.
zh: 所以这，我认为，这种情况会在整个行业越来越多地发生，基本上如果你考虑，好吧……我认为这可以发生在整个产品中，但尤其是增长团队，因为有一整套工作是微小的优化，我认为只是本质上更适合更早地解决这个。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think if you think about the lifecycle of shipping, there's four parts to it.
zh: 我认为如果你考虑交付的生命周期，它有四个部分。

speaker: Unknown
en: One is identifying opportunities.
zh: 第一是识别机会。

speaker: Unknown
en: How good is Claude at actually identifying opportunities based on different trends, based on previous trends that Claude has seen in the past?
zh: Claude 实际上根据不同的趋势识别机会有多好，根据 Claude 过去看到的以前的趋势？

speaker: Unknown
en: Second is then building the actual feature and getting it ready to ship.
zh: 第二是构建实际的功能并准备好交付。

speaker: Unknown
en: Third is testing and ensuring that it meets your quality bar and your brand bar.
zh: 第三是测试并确保它满足你的质量标准和你的品牌标准。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then fourth is then once you've actually shipped the thing, analyzing the data, gathering the learnings.
zh: 然后第四是一旦你真正交付了那个东西，分析数据，收集经验。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you think about that as the loop of, "Okay, these are four things that you can eval and hill climb on each of these areas and understand how good is a model doing for you there."
zh: 如果你把这视为那个循环，“好的，这四件事是你可以在每个领域进行评估和爬坡，并理解模型在那里为你做得有多好。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And we basically think about it in that four ways and we are scoring how good is Claude doing in each of those areas.
zh: 我们基本上以这四种方式考虑它，并且我们正在评分 Claude 在每个领域做得有多好。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so we've been testing this at pretty small scale right now.
zh: 所以我们现在以非常小的规模进行测试。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's been a lot of copy changes and some very minor UI tweaks.
zh: 这一直是大量的文案更改和一些非常微小的 UI 调整。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's delivering results and it's like you can push it, press play with it.
zh: 它正在交付结果，而且你可以推动它，按下播放键运行它。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it ultimately prints money where I'd say that the win rate is like, I would expect a senior PM to do better.

speaker: Unknown
en: I would say this is a junior PM, two, three years in.
zh: 我会说这是一个初级 PM，两三年经验。

speaker: Unknown
en: I would say this is the win rate that I would expect from a junior PM, but it's not quite at the senior PM level.
zh: 我会说这是我会期望从一个初级 PM 得到的成功率，但它还不到高级 PM 的水平。

speaker: Unknown
en: Although, I think you look at the exponential, this wasn't available at all a couple of months ago.
zh: 虽然，我认为你看那个指数级增长，几个月前这根本不可用。

speaker: Unknown
en: So it's getting better rapidly.
zh: 所以它正在快速变好。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think that it's going to change where you'll be able to do this for larger and larger types of experiments.
zh: 我认为它将改变你能够为越来越大的类型的实验做这件事的情况。

speaker: Unknown
en: But then you think about the largest types of experiments, I think that I mentioned the four pieces around identifying opportunities, building, testing and shipping.
zh: 但然后你考虑那些最大类型的实验，我认为我提到了围绕识别机会、构建、测试和交付的四个部分。

speaker: Unknown
en: The one I didn't mention there, Lenny, is cross-functional stakeholder management.
zh: 我在那里没有提到的那一个，Lenny，是跨职能利益相关者管理。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's still a need for human brains.
zh: 仍然需要人脑。

speaker: Unknown
en: There is.
zh: 是有的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think that that one is going to mean that in my eyes, the work of PMs is not going away actually, anytime soon.
zh: 我认为那一个将意味着在我眼中，PM 的工作不会消失，实际上，在不久的将来。

speaker: Unknown
en: And that piece, especially for larger projects.
zh: 而且那一部分，特别是对于更大的项目。

speaker: Unknown
en: Usually, you don't need to do as much of it for smaller stuff.
zh: 通常，你不需要为较小的东西做那么多。

speaker: Unknown
en: You can skip it.
zh: 你可以跳过它。

speaker: Unknown
en: But for larger stuff, that piece is not going away.
zh: 但对于较大的东西，那部分不会消失。

speaker: Unknown
en: Until the other stakeholders have their own little agents running around.

speaker: Unknown
en: I think that's right.
zh: 我认为那是对的。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think that would be the point where it changes.
zh: 我认为那将是它会改变的点。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's funny, we had a difficult meeting a couple of weeks ago, and me and our head of design, Joel, we were debriefing afterwards.
zh: 有趣的是，几周前我们有一个艰难的会议，我和我们的设计主管 Joel，我们在事后进行汇报。

speaker: Unknown
en: And he pings me, he's just like, "Amol, we will have AGI and it will still be impossible to get six people in a room to get to align."
zh: 他给我发消息，他就像，“Amol，我们会有 AGI，而且让六个人在房间里达成一致仍然是不可能的。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And I'm like, "Yeah, I think I can say that."
zh: 我就像，“是的，我想我可以那么说。”

speaker: Unknown
en: What's the harder alignment problem?
zh: 什么是更难的一致性问题？

speaker: Unknown
en: Oh, okay.

speaker: Unknown
en: This is so interesting and this is exactly where I feel like things are going.
zh: 这太有趣了，这确实正是我觉得事情正在发展的地方。

speaker: Unknown
en: So just to be clear what you shared here, there's basically this tool that comes up with experiments to run, to help grow Claude and all the tools.
zh: 所以只是为了澄清你在这里分享的内容，基本上这个工具提出了要运行的实验，以帮助增长 Claude 和所有工具。

speaker: Unknown
en: So it comes up with idea, somebody looks at them, approves, "Cool, let's do these things."

speaker: Unknown
en: Builds it, ships it, tests the results, see how it's doing, and then comes back of, "Here's things that are working."

speaker: Unknown
en: Is that roughly right?

en: It's roughly right.
zh: 大概是对的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And right now, we have human in the loop approving, but the amount of time, I kind of think about scale in this way of just week on week, are things getting better in each of the areas?
zh: 而现在，我们在循环中有人批准，但我以这种方式考虑规模，仅仅一周又一周，事情在每个领域变得更好了吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Are people spending less time on each of the areas?

speaker: Unknown
en: Are the results getting better in each of the areas?
zh: 每个领域的成果变得更好了吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: And as long as week on week, that's getting better, then you're like, "Okay, this whole initiative is scaling."

speaker: Unknown
en: And so that's roughly right, but you can think about it as, I think a lot of this can be automated where human review is not needed.
zh: 所以这大概是对的，但你可以把它思考为，我认为这很多可以自动化，其中不需要人工审查。

speaker: Unknown
en: So we care a lot about our brand, right?
zh: 所以我们非常关心我们的品牌，对吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: So that is something that we do look at right now.
zh: 所以那是我们现在确实看的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: We don't want to be shipping something that goes against the brand, but then you can have a skill that contains your brand guidelines and very clear, yet dos and don'ts on brand.

speaker: Unknown
en: And so all of these types of accompaniments, I think are going to get better.

speaker: Unknown
en: The model's going to get better at understanding how to use them.

speaker: Unknown
en: And so over time, I think that the need to human review this really, really decreases significantly.
zh: 所以随着时间的推移，我认为人工审查的需求真的、真的会显著减少。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, and you could always unship it if it's like, "Oh yeah, that was actually not a great idea."

speaker: Unknown
en: And that's such a good point that we think we need people to do these things forever.

speaker: Unknown
en: And it turns out, "Okay, a skill could do this really well.

speaker: Unknown
en: Here's our brand guidelines, here's our vision, here's our mission, here's our goals, here's what matters to us.

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay, no, let's not ship that thing."
zh: 好吧，不，我们不要发布那个东西。"

speaker: Unknown
en: So the reason I think this is so interesting, I've just been watching the expansion of AI doing more of the product development process, in this case the growth process of just, okay, it went from helping you write code, to writing all your code, to reviewing your code.
zh: 所以我认为这之所以如此有趣，我一直看着 AI 在产品开发过程中做得更多，在这个案例中是增长过程，就是，好吧，它从帮助你写代码，到写完你所有的代码，再到审查你的代码。

speaker: Unknown
en: Now it feels like, what are the two ends around this?

speaker: Unknown
en: It's kind of going from the middle up.

speaker: Unknown
en: The top of that is coming up with what to do and then there's the alignment stuff's still very hard.

speaker: Unknown

speaker: Unknown
en: That's a whole other thing I want to talk to you about.

speaker: Unknown

zh: 所以我在这里听到的，这也正是我认为将要开始发生的事情，是 AI 现在非常擅长告诉我们该做什么，而不仅仅是接受我们的命令并构建它。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it feels like the growth version of this is where it starts because it's so much simpler, not that growth is easy, but just like it's data-driven, there's this loop that you talk about.
zh: 感觉像这个的增长版本是它开始的地方，因为它简单得多，不是说增长容易，只是就像它是数据驱动的，有你谈论的那个循环。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I think this is such an interesting sign of things to come across just generally, product.

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.

speaker: Unknown
en: Just putting that out there.
zh: 只是把它提出来。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay.

en: So something that I'm constantly thinking about along these lines is just the future of product PM engineering, how those roles shift over time based on the stuff we're talking about.

speaker: Unknown
en: How are you working together as a triad and where do you see these roles going?
zh: 你们作为一个三人组如何协同工作，你们看到这些角色去向何方？

speaker: Unknown
en: What's most going to change, do you think, across these three roles?

speaker: Unknown
en: And it's more just a lifecycle thing of, where is the lifecycle of your team and that specific pod to identify where the bottleneck is?
zh: 这更多是一个生命周期的事情，你的团队的生命周期在哪里，那个特定的 pod，去识别瓶颈在哪里？

en: But now when things break, you need to still look at it, of, "Okay, is it the actual ratio or is there also underlying technological shifts that are also causing this to break?"
zh: 但现在当东西坏掉时，你仍然需要看它，"好吧，它是实际的比率，还是也有潜在的技术转变也在导致这个坏掉？"

speaker: Unknown
en: And so that's an interesting thing.
zh: 所以那是一件有趣的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think that smaller companies, if I'm at a 15 or 20-person company, I'm the only PM there working with some designers and engineers, I think in the smaller companies, you'll see probably the biggest blend, where the PM will be doing all sorts of ... They'll be designing, shipping, et cetera, and I think you just have extreme bifurcation.
zh: 我认为较小的公司，如果我在一家 15 或 20 人的公司，我是那里唯一的 PM，与一些设计师和工程师一起工作，我认为在较小的公司，你会看到最大的混合，PM 将做各种各样的事情……他们将在设计、发布等等，我认为你只是有极端的分化。

speaker: Unknown
en: You know, at larger organizations, more scaled organizations, I think the jury is still very much out.
zh: 你知道，在更大的组织，更多扩展的组织，我认为结论还未定。

speaker: Unknown
en: You speak to people even internally here, and different people and different teams will have different views of, "Okay, how much are these roles coming together versus how much are these roles going to be separate?"
zh: 你甚至在这里内部与人交谈，不同的人和不同的团队会有不同的看法，"好吧，这些角色在多大程度上走到一起，这些角色在多大程度上将保持分离？"

speaker: Unknown
en: I think that it's not to say even the PMs, number of PMs are shipping, and themselves shipping and pushing PRs, et cetera.
zh: 我认为这并不是说甚至 PM，PM 的数量在发布，并且他们自己在发布和推动 PR 等等。

speaker: Unknown
en: But if I look at, okay, across what I'm seeing, I think that it's clear that while PMs and designers are getting more leverage from AI, engineering is getting the most leverage right now.
zh: 但如果我看看，好吧，在我看到的里面，我认为很清楚，虽然 PM 和设计师从 AI 获得更多的杠杆，工程现在获得最多的杠杆。

speaker: Unknown
en: I look at tools like Claude Code, and the amount of leverage engineers are getting from them is higher than, I think the leverage that designers and PMs are getting from them today.
zh: 我看看像 Claude Code 这样的工具，工程师从它们那里获得的杠杆量比，我认为设计师和 PM 今天从它们那里获得的杠杆要高。

speaker: Unknown
en: Now, this is rapidly also changing, but that to me is my view today.
zh: 现在，这也在迅速改变，但这今天是我的看法。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so if you think about, okay, a default team, which is, say five engineers, one designer, one PM.
zh: 所以如果你想想，好吧，一个默认团队，比方说五个工程师，一个设计师，一个 PM。

speaker: Unknown
en: With Claude Code, that five engineers is two to 3xed.
zh: 有了 Claude Code，那五个工程师是两倍或三倍。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the PMs and designers have also increased, but now they're managing what is effectively a much larger group of engineers.
zh: 而且 PM 和设计师也增加了，但现在他们在管理一个实际上大得多的工程师群体。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so even though the headcount and the org structure hasn't changed, you're now just dealing with a situation of maybe 15 to 20 engineers in the old world, one and a half to two PMs, and maybe one and a half to two designers.
zh: 所以即使人数和组织结构没有改变，你现在只是处理一种情况，就像旧世界的 15 到 20 个工程师，一个半到两个 PM，也许一个半到两个设计师。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so we're seeing that that's putting a lot of strain on PM and design.
zh: 所以我们看到这给 PM 和设计带来了很大的压力。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it's not everywhere.
zh: 而且不是到处都是。

speaker: Unknown
en: I look at teams like Claude Code and I think that, or because that product is so technical, it's probably just the right thing, where the PMs are all basically engineers themselves anyway.
zh: 我看看像 Claude Code 这样的团队，我认为，或者因为那个产品太技术了，这可能只是正确的事情，那里的 PM 基本上都是工程师自己。

speaker: Unknown
en: But we had a product, like a PM lead on site the other week, and we were all just talking about this where across the board we're feeling this, where PM and design is just squeezed.
zh: 但我们有一个产品，就像上周现场的一位 PM 负责人，我们都在谈论这个，我们普遍都感觉到这一点，PM 和设计只是被挤压了。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's just absolutely squeezed.
zh: 只是绝对被挤压了。

speaker: Unknown
en: And we're like, "Is the right thing here, we just need to actually hire a ton more PMs?"
zh: 我们就像，"这里正确的事情是，我们实际上真的需要雇佣大量的 PM 吗？"

speaker: Unknown
en: And it could actually be where we land.
zh: 而且这实际上可能是我们要落脚的地方。

speaker: Unknown
en: On growth, how I think about it is, one, we are hiring a number of growth PMs.
zh: 在增长方面，我如何看待它，是，第一，我们正在雇佣许多增长 PM。

speaker: Unknown
en: We desperately need people who are very, very good, and we are hiring.
zh: 我们迫切需要非常、非常好的人，我们正在雇佣。

speaker: Unknown
en: So if you are excited by what we're doing and you know growth, please feel free to apply.
zh: 所以如果你对我们做的事情感到兴奋并且你懂增长，请随时申请。

speaker: Unknown
en: Would love to chat and-
zh: 很乐意聊天并且-

speaker: Unknown
en: Maybe craft an amazing cold email to you.
zh: 也许给你写一封很棒的冷邮件。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, yeah, yeah.
zh: 对，对，对。

speaker: Unknown
en: Feel free to craft that email.
zh: 请随意起草那封邮件。

speaker: Unknown
en: So that's one, is I think we are going to be hiring in a number of PMs.
zh: 所以第一点是，我认为我们将招聘多名 PM。

speaker: Unknown
en: But then the second thing that we do is we very much hire product-minded engineers.
zh: 但我们要做的第二件事是，我们非常热衷于招聘具备产品思维的工程师。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think this has always been the best thing to do in growth.
zh: 我认为这在增长中一直都是最好的做法。

speaker: Unknown
en: You always want to have the engineers coming up with ideas, et cetera, especially people who can really step in as mini PMs if the PM is absent.
zh: 你总是希望工程师能提出想法等等，特别是那些在 PM 缺席时能真正作为迷你 PM 顶上的人。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so we're basically more formally leveraging that right now because we are so stretched.
zh: 所以我们现在基本上是在更正式地利用这一点，因为我们非常捉襟见肘。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, the frame that we have is that if a project is two weeks of engineering time or less, then the engineer is on the hook to effectively be the PM for that.
zh: 所以，我们的框架是，如果一个项目的工程时间不超过两周，那么工程师就要有效地充当该项目的 PM。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so that means things like talking to security, talking to legal, talking to cross-functional stakeholders, and the engineer is very much driving that.
zh: 这意味着诸如与安全部门交谈、与法务交谈、与跨职能干系人交谈，而且工程师在很大程度上在推动这件事。

speaker: Unknown
en: The PM will get looped in and they'll advise if needed.
zh: PM 会被拉进来，如果需要会提供建议。

speaker: Unknown
en: And if something's wildly going off track, then they'll step in, but they're much more in an advisory capacity versus execution.
zh: 如果事情严重偏离轨道，他们会介入，但他们更多的是处于顾问角色而非执行角色。

speaker: Unknown
en: If a project is more than two engineering weeks, then the default is that the PM should continue to be on the hook for making that go well.
zh: 如果一个项目超过两周工程时间，那么默认情况下 PM 应继续负责确保事情顺利进行。

speaker: Unknown
en: They'll still delegate more to eng, but they're squarely accountable.
zh: 他们仍然会向工程部门下放更多权力，但他们负完全责任。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's not fully clean-cut.
zh: 这并非完全泾渭分明。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's like, use your head.
zh: 就像是，动动脑子。

speaker: Unknown
en: If this is a one-week thing, but it's extremely controversial, the PM should probably still drive it.
zh: 如果这是一周的工作，但极具争议性，PM 可能仍然应该主导它。

speaker: Unknown
en: But that, I think is the approach that I expect more companies will start to do, which is just deputize the engineers to be mini PMs.
zh: 但这，我认为是我预期更多公司会开始采用的做法，也就是授权工程师成为迷你 PM。

speaker: Unknown
en: Now, not everyone can do it.
zh: 现在，不是每个人都能做到这一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: So the PMs, the engineers who are more product-minded, suddenly their value goes up significantly in order of magnitude.
zh: 所以那些 PM 们，那些更有产品思维的工程师，他们的价值突然大幅上升了一个数量级。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then I think we will probably still be hiring a lot of PMs.
zh: 然后我想我们可能还是会招聘很多 PM。

speaker: Unknown
en: There is so much interesting stuff I want to follow up on here.
zh: 这里我想跟进的有趣点太多了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay, so one is this idea of two weeks, just briefly.
zh: 好的，首先是这个两周的概念，简单说一下。

speaker: Unknown
en: I always joke as a PM, you can go on vacation and be away for a couple of weeks from your team and things are going to be all right.
zh: 我作为 PM 总是开玩笑说，你可以去度个假，离开团队几周，一切都会好好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's a momentum, there's a plan, people keep operating, and it feels like that's kind of this rule of thumb you use of just, "Okay, if it's a two-week project, you'll be all right without a PM.
zh: 有一种势头，有一个计划，人们继续运作，感觉就像是你使用的这个经验法则，"好的，如果是两周的项目，没有 PM 你也会没事的。

speaker: Unknown
en: You can handle it."
zh: 你能搞定它。"

speaker: Unknown
en: I love that those two connect.
zh: 我喜欢这两者之间的联系。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay.
zh: 好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: The other here is so interesting.
zh: 另一点非常有趣。

speaker: Unknown
en: So you're saying here that because engineers are so accelerated, and this all makes sense, PMs and design are kind of just like, "Holy."
zh: 所以你在这里说，因为工程师加速得这么厉害，这一切都说得通，PM 和设计基本上就像是，"天哪。"

speaker: Unknown
en: It feels like hard to keep up with this pace of engineering.
zh: 感觉很难跟上这种工程节奏。

speaker: Unknown
en: And what you're saying is you need more and more PMs to keep up.
zh: 而你的意思是，你需要越来越多的 PM 才能跟上。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's one route, or it's engineers that can PM essentially, which is so funny.
zh: 这是一条路，或者由能做 PM 工作的工程师来，这太搞笑了。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's just like, okay, great news for the product managers until more of the PMey stuff can be done by AI.
zh: 就像是，好的，这对产品经理来说是个好消息，直到更多 PM 的工作可以由 AI 完成。

speaker: Unknown
en: But that's a really interesting trend.
zh: 但这是一个非常有趣的趋势。

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't know, is there anything else there just like, "Oh wow, we actually may need more PMs"?
zh: 我不知道，还有别的吗，就像是，"哦哇，我们实际上可能需要更多 PM"？

speaker: Unknown
en: The ratio of more PMs, fewer engineers might be the future.
zh: 更多 PM、更少工程师的比例可能是未来。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think it just really depends on the industry, the size of company.
zh: 我认为这真的取决于行业、公司规模。

speaker: Unknown
en: Any company where you're building something that's much more developer-focused, you're going to rely on the engineers a lot more.
zh: 任何你构建更面向开发者的产品的公司，你会更依赖工程师。

speaker: Unknown
en: Earlier companies, you don't have as much of this cross-functional coordination, stakeholder alignment nonsense that you need all these PMs for, so you can get by with less.
zh: 早期公司，你没有那么多这种跨职能协调、干系人对齐的无稽之谈，你需要所有这些 PM 来做这些，所以你可以用更少的 PM 过得去。

speaker: Unknown
en: But then as a company scales, and if I think about, okay, now you have this ratio where maybe the one PM became two PMs from productivity, that the five engineers became 20 engineers, the one designer maybe became three designers.
zh: 但随着公司扩张，如果我想一想，好的，现在你有这个比例，也许一个 PM 因为效率变成了两个 PM，五个工程师变成了二十个工程师，一个设计师可能变成了三个设计师。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you think about what is the best use of time for that PM, I think this is a really interesting thing, of how much should PMs be actually shipping things themselves versus everything else?
zh: 如果你思考什么是那个 PM 时间的最佳利用方式，我认为这是一个非常有趣的事情，PM 实际上应该自己交付多少东西，还是做其他所有事情？

speaker: Unknown
en: I think in the world where you're limited on engineering, the PM should definitely be shipping things.
zh: 我认为在你受工程限制的世界里，PM 应该绝对应该亲自交付东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think in today's world, it's a good way to get an understanding of the tools, which is really important.
zh: 我认为在当今世界，这是了解工具的一种好方式，这非常重要。

speaker: Unknown
en: So the PM should be shipping for that reason.
zh: 因此 PM 应该出于这个原因去交付。

speaker: Unknown
en: But if I'm one PM or two PMs and there's 20 engineers, I think about what is the incremental value I can add with my time?
zh: 但如果我是两个 PM 之一，而有二十个工程师，我会思考我的时间能增加的增量价值是什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: And is it actually shipping the 21st PM feature or is it saying, "How am I getting a little bit better at guiding the team on what the right opportunities are?"
zh: 而且实际上是交付第 21 个 PM 功能，还是说，"我如何在引导团队找到正确机会方面做得更好一点？"

speaker: Unknown
en: And so that's where I think in this world, you may have all these engineers who are like mini PMs, and the better that happens, that's where I would love to be doing more of.
zh: 所以这就是我认为在这个世界里，你可能会拥有所有这些像迷你 PM 一样的工程师，而这种情况发生得越好，我就越希望能做更多这样的事。

speaker: Unknown
en: But still, if you then get a really good PM who can come in and can improve that, the why and the what particularly by 5%, that is such a high leverage higher.
zh: 但是，如果你随后能有一位真正优秀的 PM 进来并能改进这一点，改进"为什么"和"是什么"，特别是改进 5%，那就是如此高杠杆的收益。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is such an interesting insight you're making.
zh: 你提出的这个见解真是太有趣了。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's so counter to how a lot of people are thinking PM is evolving.
zh: 这与很多人认为 PM 正在演进的方式截然不同。

speaker: Unknown
en: What I'm hearing here is, because PMs are so behind, because engineers are just getting so much done, there's many people here, "Okay, you need to be prototyping, you need to be shipping PRs as a PM."
zh: 我在这里听到的是，因为 PM 们太落后了，因为工程师们的产出太多了，这里有很多人，说：“好吧，你需要制作原型，你需要作为 PM 提交 PR。”

speaker: Unknown
en: What you're saying, which I completely agree with, is your time is much better spent helping PM basically, and helping the engineers become better PMs themselves.
zh: 你所说的，我完全赞同，是你的时间最好花在帮助 PM 基本上，以及帮助工程师们自己成为更好的 PM。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the leverage there is a lot higher than you spending time coding, shipping PRs in most cases.
zh: 而且那里的杠杆比你在大多数情况下花时间写代码、提交 PR 要高得多。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think that's true in certain circumstances.
zh: 我认为这在某些情况下是正确的。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think that as a smaller company, I don't know that that's the case.
zh: 我认为作为一家较小的公司，我不确定那是真的。

speaker: Unknown
en: In a smaller company where it's all hands on deck, I think you probably need to be shipping.
zh: 在一家较小公司里，那里是全员上阵，我认为你可能需要去发布（产品）。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think if you're in a company where budgets are very tight, and engineers are very tight, and you're not able to just hire because money's unlimited, then you need to do what is needed to accelerate the impact your team's going to have.
zh: 我认为如果你在一家预算非常紧张、工程师非常紧张，而且你无法仅仅因为钱无限就招人的公司，那么你需要做任何需要做的事情来加速你的团队将产生的影响力。

speaker: Unknown
en: Right?
zh: 对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: So there's going to be a number of cases, where as a PM, the right thing to do is to be like, "Screw it.
zh: 所以会有很多情况，作为 PM，正确的做法就像是，“去它的。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm shipping and I'm going it the whole way."
zh: 我要发布产品，并且我会一路做到底。”

speaker: Unknown
en: But I'm talking here more about the larger companies, more scaled businesses.
zh: 但我在这里更多谈论的是大型公司，更大规模的企业。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, if you have 20 engineers, is it the highest use leverage of your time to ship an extra feature, or figure out how do I up-level everything that we're doing, get the user insights better, et cetera?
zh: 是的，如果你有 20 个工程师，你花时间发布一个额外功能是你时间最高效的利用吗？还是弄清楚如何提升我们所做的一切，获得更好的用户洞察等等？

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think there's also an element of shipping to learn, building a prototype so that you can have a better opinion.
zh: 我认为这里也有通过发布来学习的要素，制作一个原型以便你能有一个更好的观点。

speaker: Unknown
en: A lot of people talk about just, "I'm just going to try three things, see how it goes and that'll help inform the roadmap."
zh: 很多人只是谈论，“我只是要尝试三件事，看看结果如何，这将有助于提供路线图的信息。”

speaker: Unknown
en: This is now the PRD, is look at it instead of talking about it.
zh: 这就是现在的 PRD，是看着它而不是谈论它。

speaker: Unknown
en: So there's a lot of value there still.
zh: 所以那里仍然有很多价值。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's very true.
zh: 那非常正确。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think that's a great point.
zh: 我认为那很棒。

speaker: Unknown
en: So even for me now where we've got number of PMs, we've got many engineers, there are certain times when I'm like, "I want to articulate the idea I have in my head."
zh: 所以即使对现在的我来说，我们有很多 PM，我们有很多工程师，在某些时候我会像，“我想要阐明我脑海中的想法。”

speaker: Unknown
en: It's just better for me to prototype it and show it.
zh: 对我来说把它原型化并展示出来会更好。

speaker: Unknown
en: Right?
zh: 对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: So I think that is really important.
zh: 所以我认为这真的很重要。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then we are very scrappy.
zh: 然后我们非常拼。

speaker: Unknown
en: So we're a big company by name, valuation, but we're extremely scrappy, and just the focus internally is just minimize bureaucracy and just go.
zh: 所以我们在名义上是一家大公司，估值也是，但我们极其拼，而且内部的重点只是最大限度地减少官僚主义，只管去做。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so probably 70%, maybe 60, 70, 80% of what we ship does not have a PRD.
zh: 所以可能我们发布（产品）的 70%，也许 60，70，80% 并没有 PRD。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm averse to PRDs.
zh: 我反感 PRD。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think I just hate documentation.
zh: 我想我只是讨厌文档。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm just like, "Go, go, go.
zh: 我只是像，“去，去，去。

speaker: Unknown
en: Just cut the blockers."
zh: 只管移除障碍。”

speaker: Unknown
en: 20, 30% of stuff where it's like, it's important, it's really important to get right.
zh: 对于 20，30% 的东西，那里它是重要的，做对它真的很重要。

speaker: Unknown
en: The documentation should be really good and that people should spend a lot of time on it.
zh: 文档应该真的很好，人们应该花很多时间在上面。

speaker: Unknown
en: But by and large, I think PRDs are just outdated at this point, and you can just kick things off with a good team without needing to do that sort of thing.
zh: 但总的来说，我认为 PRD 在这一点上已经过时了，你可以只管和一个好的团队开始做事，而不需要做那种事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: Say more about that.
zh: 多说一点关于那个。

speaker: Unknown
en: What do you do to help make sure, because people can build so fast, or spend a lot of time going in the wrong direction, ship things that are not what you're asking?
zh: 你做什么来帮助确保，因为人们可以构建得如此之快，或者花很多时间走在错误的方向上，发布的不是你要求的东西？

speaker: Unknown
en: How do you kick off a project and clarify, "Here's what we're doing"?
zh: 你如何启动一个项目并阐明，“这就是我们在做的”？

speaker: Unknown
en: Is it just a conversation or is there anything beyond that?
zh: 这只是一个对话还是除了那个还有别的什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: It really depends on the size of it.
zh: 这真的取决于它的大小。

speaker: Unknown
en: And this kind of goes back to the two-week thing.
zh: 这有点回到了那个两周的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: So why we have that two-week thing, it's more like, how do I have some filter to say if we're investing heavily into something, we should apply more thinking behind it versus if it's a smaller set of investments, just go for it?
