speaker: Unknown
en: I think internally, we are very excited and I think, honestly, we're very, very optimistic about where this can go, but we also understand what the risks are.
zh: 我认为在内部,我们非常兴奋,而且我认为,老实说,我们非常非常乐观它能发展到哪里,但我们也明白风险是什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so for us, that top line objective of, " This needs to go well for humanity.
zh: 所以对我们来说,那个首要的宏观目标,就是「这对人类来说必须进展顺利。

speaker: Unknown
en: This just needs to go well for humanity," that is something that we are happy to take a significant commercial hit for.
zh: 这对人类来说必须进展顺利」,这是我们要愿意为此承受重大商业打击的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: And we've done that time and time again, right?
zh: 我们一次又一次地这样做,对吧?

speaker: Unknown
en: So we have it like back then, okay, you had Claude, but you don't want to release it because you're like, there's these safety risks.
zh: 所以我们有就像回到那时,好吧,你有了 Claude,但你不想发布它,因为你觉得有这些安全风险。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so there's time and time again that I've seen, we've been happy to take that hit and it's actually worked out well for us in other ways.
zh: 所以一次又一次我看到,我们愿意承受那个打击,而实际上这以其他方式对我们很有利。

speaker: Unknown
en: From like a growth lens, I look at it as like, growth teams can often push the boundaries of what is good user X, UX, et cetera, because they're trying to very, very much eke out metrics at times.
zh: 从像增长视角来看,我把它看作是,增长团队经常可以突破什么是好的用户 X,用户体验等的界限,因为他们有时试图非常非常地榨取指标。

speaker: Unknown
en: When I think about if a controversial test is brought to me, I sort of look at it as like, there's two types of tests that are controversial.
zh: 当我想如果一个有争议的测试提交给我时,我有点把它看作是,有两种类型的有争议的测试。

speaker: Unknown
en: One is when that test is so controversial that you just should not run the thing, because the results don't matter because you would not ship it for a combination of brand and sort of customer friendliness and values.
zh: 一种是当那个测试是如此有争议以至于你就不应该运行这个东西,因为结果不重要,因为你出于品牌和某种客户友好度以及价值观的组合而不会发布它。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then the second is where it's like, it's controversial.
zh: 然后第二种是就像,它有争议。

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't like it.
zh: 我不喜欢它。

speaker: Unknown
en: I certainly don't love it, but it's not like a red line.
zh: 我当然不爱它,但这不像是一条红线。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so you're like, if someone comes to you with conviction and is like, "I have a really good hypothesis around this," and you're like, "I don't love it, but you can run the test and see what impact it has."
zh: 所以你就像,如果有人带着信念来找你并且说,「我对此有一个非常好的假设」,而你就像,「我不爱它,但你可以运行这个测试看看它有什么影响。」

speaker: Unknown
en: And if it's a high level of cringe or ick, then I want to see a high level of return for the result for that.
zh: 如果它是高度尴尬或恶心的,那么我想看到那个结果有高水平的回报。

speaker: Unknown
en: I kind of think about everything and like, is it in one or is it in two?
zh: 我有点思考所有事情就像,它是在一类还是在二类?

speaker: Unknown
en: And for every company, that one and two is different.
zh: 对于每家公司,那一类和二类是不同的。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think for us, AI safety is very much in one.
zh: 我认为对我们来说,AI 安全非常在一类里。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think that it's like, "Yeah, that's why we exist.
zh: 我认为就像,「是的,那就是我们要存在的原因。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, it's fine.
zh: 是的,没问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: We're just not going to do this thing."
zh: 我们就是不打算做这件事。」

speaker: Unknown
en: I think then, there's other things that fall into two, where you're like, heightened sensitivity, but we can try it and see what happens.
zh: 我认为然后,还有其他事情落入二类,你就像,高度敏感,但我们可以尝试它看看会发生什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think zooming out though, Lenny, one of the biggest mistakes I feel like I see growth teams make, and particularly just hardcore growth practitioners, is just trying to squeeze every last dollar.
zh: 我认为拉远来看,不过,Lenny,我觉得我看到的增长团队犯的最大错误之一,特别是只是硬核的增长从业者,就是只是试图榨取每一分钱。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think this is like a general principle also in life.
zh: 我认为这也是生活中的一个普遍原则。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you're a founder raising money, you're just trying to squeeze that last dollar.
zh: 如果你是一个正在融资的创始人,你只是试图榨取最后一块钱。

speaker: Unknown
en: You don't want to do that because you want people to come back next time as well, is my view.
zh: 你不想那样做,因为你希望人们下次也回来,这是我的观点。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think in growth, I think it's really important that you just need to be okay leaving money on the table.
zh: 而且我认为在增长中,我认为非常重要的一点是你需要对于把钱留在桌上感到没问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: And that's a core principle for us as a growth team, where we are very comfortable for going metric impact in order to prioritize safety, in order to protect our brand, in order to hold a high quality bar and to maintain a great user experience.
zh: 这是我们作为增长团队的一个核心原则,我们非常愿意为了优先考虑安全,为了保护我们的品牌,为了保持高标准质量以及维持良好的用户体验而牺牲指标影响。

speaker: Unknown
en: And if you look beyond the short term and like, "Okay, what are the numbers for this quarter?"
zh: 如果你超越短期来看就像,「好吧,这个季度的数字是多少?」

speaker: Unknown
en: And you zoom out and you think about what are the very best products out there, you realize this is how they all operate.
zh: 然后你拉远视角思考一下那里最好的产品是什么,你意识到它们都是这样运作的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And that's actually the thing that's going to drive more growth long-term as well.
zh: 而那实际上是将在长期推动更多增长的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so I think that actually ties back to safety, where as the risks get higher and the stakes get higher, I think the fact that we are taking a stance and safety is critical to what we do, is actually going to become a significant, it's going to be a significant competitive advantage for us that I think is going to help us in the long run.
zh: 所以我认为这实际上回扣到安全,随着风险变高和赌注变大,我认为我们采取立场和安全对我们所做事情至关重要这一事实,实际上将成为一个重要的,我认为这将成为我们在长期中帮助我们的重要竞争优势。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And as you share all this, clearly it's working.
zh: 当你分享这一切时,显然它是有效的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Anthropic is killing it.
zh: Anthropic 表现得非常好。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I love when someone doing something, someone approaching the problem that way, it actually works out.
zh: 所以我喜欢当有人这样做,有人以这种方式处理问题,它实际上成功了。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's the same way I think about with my newsletter.
zh: 这就像我想我的通讯的方式一样。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's so much more I can do to grow it.
zh: 还有更多我可以做的事情来增长它。

speaker: Unknown
en: My philosophy is just focus on creating good content, nothing else.
zh: 我的哲学只是专注于创造好的内容,没有别的。

speaker: Unknown
en: All these micro optimizations are not going to matter in the end.
zh: 所有这些微观优化最终都不重要。

speaker: Unknown
en: It'll grow through people sharing it if it's useful to them.
zh: 如果它对他们有用,它会通过人们分享它而增长。

speaker: Unknown
en: And a very small scale, I have similar philosophy.
zh: 而且在非常小的规模上,我有类似的哲学。

speaker: Unknown
en: One of the things that people are probably thinking about as we talk, we joke about AI replacing parts of jobs here and there.
zh: 人们可能在当我们谈话时正在思考的事情之一,我们开玩笑说 AI 在这里和那里取代工作的一部分。

speaker: Unknown
en: It is pretty scary to a lot of people, just like, "What is the future of my job?
zh: 这对很多人来说很可怕,就像,「我工作的未来是什么?

speaker: Unknown
en: Will I have a job?
zh: 我会有工作吗?

speaker: Unknown
en: How do I stay relevant in this future?"
zh: 我如何在这个未来保持相关性?」

speaker: Unknown
en: So there's a couple of questions here.
zh: 这里有几个问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: One is just like, for folks that want to thrive in this approaching AI future, as a PM, as a growth person, do you have any advice, things that they should be doing right now?
zh: 一个是，对于那些想要在这个即将到来的 AI 未来中茁壮成长的人，无论是作为 PM 还是作为增长人员，你有什么建议，有什么他们现在应该做的事情吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: To me, a couple of things come up.
zh: 对我来说，有几件事冒了出来。

speaker: Unknown
en: One thing that everyone says, is use the tools, you need to be on top of the tools.
zh: 大家都说的一件事是，使用工具，你需要紧跟工具。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think you need to be using Claude Code, you need to be using Cowork and understanding just each model release.
zh: 我认为你需要使用 Claude Code，你需要使用 Cowork 并且仅仅了解每一次模型发布。

speaker: Unknown
en: What is the new things you can do with this?
zh: 你可以用它做什么新事情？

speaker: Unknown
en: How can you apply this to your job?
zh: 你如何将其应用到你的工作中？

speaker: Unknown
en: It'll work well in some things, it'll work terribly in other things.
zh: 它在一些事情上会运作良好，在其他事情上会运作得很糟糕。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then the model launched later, it's like, "Oh shit, that other thing worked."
zh: 然后当模型稍后发布时，就像，“噢，该死，那个其他东西起作用了。”

speaker: Unknown
en: But if you didn't go back to try it, you would not have known.
zh: 但如果你没有回去尝试它，你就不会知道。

speaker: Unknown
en: And now, many months have passed and you didn't know that that was possible.
zh: 而现在，好几个月过去了，你不知道那是可能的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so I think that being on top of the tools is really important, both for improving your own productivity, but also for getting product sense around AI products, which I think is just going to become increasingly important.
zh: 所以我认为紧跟工具真的非常重要，既为了提高你自己的生产力，也为了获得对 AI 产品的产品感，我认为这只会变得越来越重要。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think then, zooming out beyond that, I look at it as like, just leaning into where you'll have a competitive advantage and an unfair advantage.
zh: 我认为然后，从那放大来看，我把它看作，只是倾向于你将拥有竞争优势和不对称优势的地方。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so to me, there are some people, some PMs who are really good at like, craft, for example.
zh: 所以对我来说，有一些人，一些 PM，他们真的擅长，比如说，手艺。

speaker: Unknown
en: And there's others who you'll throw them into a situation with all these stakeholders who have all these strong opinions and there's no way they're going to mediate this.
zh: 还有其他人，如果你把他们扔进一个有一堆利益相关者的处境，这些人都有强烈的意见，根本没有办法让他们去调解。

speaker: Unknown
en: And they come out and it's like, everyone's swimming in the right direction.
zh: 他们出来时，就像，每个人都在正确的方向上游动。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so if you think about like, what is the major skillset that you have where you spike that can be tied to delivering, driving impact for a company in a product role, I would just double down on that and almost forget the weaknesses.
zh: 所以如果你想想，你拥有的主要技能组合是什么，你在哪里可以成为尖子，可以与在产品角色中为公司交付、驱动影响联系在一起，我就只会加强那个，几乎忘记弱点。

speaker: Unknown
en: Just what can you do to become the best person at that thing, because that's very, very valuable.
zh: 就是你能做什么来成为在那件事上最好的人，因为那是非常非常有价值的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think that ties to the notion of just leaning into being interdisciplinary.
zh: 我认为这与倾向于成为跨学科的概念有关。

speaker: Unknown
en: So going back to some of what we mentioned earlier on where it's like, "Okay, in this world of engineers are mini PMs, that the engineer who is highly product-minded is a unicorn, is an absolute unicorn."
zh: 所以回到我们早些时候提到的一些东西，就像，“好吧，在这个工程师是迷你 PM 的世界里，那个非常有产品思维的工程师是独角兽，是绝对的独角兽。”

speaker: Unknown
en: I think the same thing is true for PMs, where it's like, "Okay, now, in this ratio, if the designer's really stretched and you're a PM who can design, you are also a unicorn now."
zh: 我认为对 PM 来说也是一样，就像，“好吧，现在，在这个比例下，如果设计师真的忙不过来，而你是一个可以做设计的 PM，你现在也是独角兽。”

speaker: Unknown
en: The chances of a company letting you go has gone down dramatically because you are now just so much more useful.
zh: 公司解雇你的几率已经急剧下降了，因为你现在更有用了。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so I think that is just really, really important.
zh: 所以我认为这真的非常重要。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think if I look at what's benefited me, it's probably a version of this.
zh: 我认为如果我看看什么让我受益，那可能是这个的一个版本。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think that for me, I came from a founder background.
zh: 我认为对我来说，我来自创始人背景。

speaker: Unknown
en: So the mix of the founder background, the finance.
zh: 所以是创始人背景、金融的混合。

speaker: Unknown
en: I was an investment banker, so the finance background and numbers.
zh: 我是一名投资银行家，所以是金融背景和数字。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then sales, I almost became an account exec, instead of going into product.
zh: 然后是销售，我差点成了一个客户经理，而不是进入产品领域。

speaker: Unknown
en: I was right on the edge of, "Should I go into sales or product?"
zh: 我就在边缘上，“我应该进入销售还是产品？”

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think a combination of those, along with growth is probably what's led to like, there are certain areas and situations where I can just have outsized impact to other people.
zh: 我认为这些的结合，加上增长，可能导致了有某些领域和处境，我可以相比其他人拥有不成比例的影响。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so, understanding what that is for you is really important.
zh: 所以，理解那对你来说是什么真的非常重要。

speaker: Unknown
en: I look at our financial services product.
zh: 我看看我们的金融服务产品。

speaker: Unknown
en: We've launched Claude for Sheets, Claude for Excel-
zh: 我们推出了 Claude for Sheets，Claude for Excel——

speaker: Unknown
en: They kind of like, tanked the market, by the way.
zh: 顺便说一下，它们几乎像是占领了市场。

speaker: Unknown
en: Everyone went crazy.
zh: 大家都疯了。

speaker: Unknown
en: I know.
zh: 我知道。

speaker: Unknown
en: But the guy running that, Nick Lin, he came from investment banking, he came from private equity, and he just has such a competitive advantage where he is building that product and he's like, "I know this, I know this."
zh: 但是负责那个的人，Nick Lin，他来自投资银行，他来自私募股权，他只是拥有这么大的竞争优势，他在构建那个产品，他就像，“我知道这个，我知道这个。”

speaker: Unknown
en: He's built for this.
zh: 他为此而生。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so I think just understanding what are those interdisciplinary areas you can lean into, to make yourself more just higher impact is really important.
zh: 所以我认为只是理解你可以倾向于哪些跨学科领域，让你自己更有影响力真的非常重要。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then the last one is just being adaptable.
zh: 然后最后一点就是适应性强。

speaker: Unknown
en: Anyone who is trying to just keep applying old playbooks, I think you're going to make life a lot harder for yourself.
zh: 任何试图只是继续应用旧剧本的人，我认为你会让生活变得艰难得多。

speaker: Unknown
en: So one of the biggest things you come into Anthropic, is you need to understand that probably 50, 60, 70% of how you operate in the past, just throw it out the door.
zh: 所以当你进入 Anthropic 时最大的事情之一是，你需要理解可能 50%，60%，70% 你过去运作的方式，就把它扔出门吧。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's not going to be relevant.
zh: 它不相关了。

speaker: Unknown
en: And if you try to stick to that, you're going to have a lot of friction and it's not going to be helpful.
zh: 如果你试图坚持那个，你会有很多摩擦，它不会有帮助。

speaker: Unknown
en: So just being adaptable and understanding, "Okay, the job's changed this way, I'm going to go that way," is I think like, it's so, so important.
zh: 所以只是适应性强并理解，“好吧，工作变成了这样，我要走那条路，”我认为这非常非常重要。

speaker: Unknown
en: That is awesome advice.
zh: 那是很棒的建议。

speaker: Unknown
en: It matches a lot of what Jenny shared when she came on the podcast, the design leader on Claude and Cowork and all these things, of just the idea of going deep, becoming the best or one of the best at a very specific thing, and that not every company will need and you don't need all 10, but just going deep on something.
zh: 这与 Jenny 分享的很多东西相符，当她来到播客时，她是 Claude 和 Cowork 以及所有这些东西的设计负责人，关于深入的想法，成为最好的人或非常擅长某件事的人之一，而且不是每家公司都需要，你不需要全部 10 项，只是在某件事上深入。

speaker: Unknown
en: I forget how she described it.
zh: 我忘了她是怎么描述它的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Marc Andreessen said the same thing.
zh: Marc Andreessen 也说了同样的话。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think we called it a sideways E, instead of just T-shaped of one thing.
zh: 我们称之为横向 E，而不是仅仅是一个东西的 T 型。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you could have a couple of things you're really, really good at, there's a lot of power to that.
zh: 如果你能有几件事是你真的、非常擅长的,那这里面有很大的力量。

speaker: Unknown
en: Oh, man.
zh: 噢,天哪。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay.
zh: 好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Before I get into something that I think will blow a lot of people's minds about how we actually met and kind of a big part of your journey, let me just ask you this.
zh: 在我谈到一些我认为会让很多人大吃一惊的事情之前,关于我们实际上是如何认识的以及你旅程中相当重要的一部分,让我先问你这个问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: Is there anything else about Anthropic that might be worth sharing, might be worth talking about?
zh: 关于 Anthropic 还有什么其他值得分享的、值得谈论的事情吗?

speaker: Unknown
en: The thing that maybe comes to mind is just our culture and the people that we have here.
zh: 我想到了的也许是我们的文化以及我们这里的人。

speaker: Unknown
en: I really think it's our secret sauce.
zh: 我真的认为那是我们的秘制酱料。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think it's the thing that is the most defensible, the thing that no one else is going to be able to replicate.
zh: 我认为那是最具防御性的东西,是其他人无法复制的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I don't think it's an accident.
zh: 而且我不认为这是个意外。

speaker: Unknown
en: Leadership has really invested in this a lot.
zh: 领导层真的在这方面投入了很多。

speaker: Unknown
en: And Daniela and Dario, they really believe in this a lot, and I think they've just created a very special culture.
zh: Daniela 和 Dario,他们真的非常相信这一点,我认为他们只是创造了一种非常特别的文化。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I would say that this is truly a mission-driven company and I was not 100% sure of that when I joined.
zh: 所以我想说这真的是一家使命驱动的公司,当我加入时我并不是 100% 确定的。

speaker: Unknown
en: I was like, I think that this is an exciting company.
zh: 我当时想,我认为这是一家令人兴奋的公司。

speaker: Unknown
en: I very much agree with their principles, but I didn't know anyone at the company.
zh: 我非常赞同他们的原则,但我在公司里并不认识任何人。

speaker: Unknown
en: I didn't have any references on what it was like inside.
zh: 我没有任何关于内部情况是怎样的参考。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I was a little skeptical when I joined.
zh: 所以我加入时有点怀疑。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm like, "At least they're talking about it, but I don't know.
zh: 我想,「至少他们在谈论这个,但我不知道。

speaker: Unknown
en: Are they serious about this?"
zh: 他们是认真的吗?」

speaker: Unknown
en: And then I came in very early on, and I was like, "Oh, oh shit.
zh: 然后我很早就进来了,我想,「噢,噢该死。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay.
zh: 好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Maybe they're even more serious about this internally.
zh: 也许他们在内部甚至更认真地对待这一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: They talk externally."
zh: 他们在对外谈论。」

speaker: Unknown
en: And so it's a mission-driven company where people viscerally, viscerally understand both the upsides and the downsides of the technology, and therefore, understand the divergent ranges of how this may go for humanity and how different of a future that could be.
zh: 所以这是一家使命驱动的公司,人们发自内心地、发自内心地理解技术的利弊,因此,理解这对人类可能产生的影响的分歧范围,以及那可能是一个多么不同的未来。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think when you understand that, of how different this could be as a future for all of us and our children and our grandchildren, et cetera, I think it leads to a lot of passion for what we do and it just leads to a lot of belief in what we're doing.
zh: 我认为当你理解了这一点,理解了这对我们所有人、我们的孩子、我们的孙辈等等来说可能有多么不同,我认为这会导致对我们所做事情的巨大热情,并导致对我们正在做的事情的巨大信念。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so I look at every other job I've had in the past, and there's some degree of people at the company who are just checked out, where it's like, "I'm here, I'm sick of this, but I don't have a better option or I'm getting paid too much to leave, et cetera."
zh: 所以我回顾我过去拥有的每一份工作,总有一些程度的人只是不在状态了,就像是,「我在这里,我受够了这个,但我没有更好的选择,或者我薪水太高了不能离开,等等。」

speaker: Unknown
en: That is just not the case here.
zh: 但在这里情况并非如此。

speaker: Unknown
en: I have not met a single person.
zh: 我没遇到过一个人。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm saying a single person, who has checked out.
zh: 我是说,没有一个人,是处于不在状态的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Everyone is putting everything they have on the table.
zh: 每个人都把拥有的一切都摆在了台面上。

speaker: Unknown
en: Everyone is pouring it out and leaving nothing behind and is just fully, fully in it.
zh: 每个人都在倾尽全力,不留任何余地,只是完全、完全地投入其中。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so I think that leads to like, releases energy that is just very, very hard to describe.
zh: 所以我认为这会导致一种非常、非常难以描述的释放能量。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think you have that energy, you have that mission-driven nature, and then it's such an open culture.
zh: 我认为你有那种能量,你有那种使命驱动的特质,然后它是这样一种开放的文化。

speaker: Unknown
en: So leadership is very, very transparent with us on things.

speaker: Unknown
en: Slack, it's a whole maze.

speaker: Unknown
en: We're very open.

en: Everyone has these notebook channels where you have your own Twitter feed in a way, where you're just talking about your thoughts about things.
zh: 每个人都有这些 notebook 频道,在某种程度上你拥有自己的 Twitter 信息流,你只是在谈论你对事情的想法。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so you can go and join the Slack channel, the notebook channels of people on research and all these other areas, and you can learn whatever you want and you can spend so much time getting lost in that as well.
zh: 所以你可以去加入 Slack 频道,加入研究和所有这些其他领域的人的 notebook 频道,你可以学习任何你想学的东西,你也可以花很多时间沉迷其中。

speaker: Unknown
en: But that openness where we even encouraged people can just argue with Dario.
zh: 但这种开放性,甚至鼓励人们可以和 Dario 争论。

speaker: Unknown
en: There was an all hands.
zh: 有一次全员大会。

speaker: Unknown
en: You said something where someone didn't agree, and the person goes onto Dario's notebook channel and just says, "Hey, I didn't appreciate how you said this and this and that."
zh: 你说了一些某人不同意的话,那个人就去了 Dario 的 notebook 频道,只是说,「嘿,我不喜欢你这样这样说和那样说。」

speaker: Unknown
en: And then it sparked a whole big debate.
zh: 然后它引发了一场大辩论。

speaker: Unknown
en: But that sort of thing where it's encouraged like, "Go to leadership and disagree with them, challenge them publicly," I think that just leads to a level of trust.
zh: 但那种事情是被鼓励的,比如,「去找领导层并不同意他们的观点,公开挑战他们」,我认为这只会导致一种信任水平。

speaker: Unknown
en: And all of that together, I think it just means that we have this very, very deep sense of togetherness that, man, I've never experienced anything like it.
zh: 所有这一切加在一起,我认为这只是意味着我们有这种非常、非常深厚的在一起的感觉,伙计,我从未经历过这样的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then you get to the talent, right?
zh: 然后你到了人才层面,对吧?

speaker: Unknown
en: I think that is the thing where the talent density is like, I feel like I'm playing for Real Madrid at times.
zh: 我认为这就是那种人才密度就像,我觉得有时候我是在为皇家马德里效力。

speaker: Unknown
en: I look around, I'm like, "Man, I'm playing for Madrid," where it's like, you just have the best people in the world.
zh: 我环顾四周,我想,「伙计,我在为皇马踢球」,就像是,你只是拥有世界上最好的人。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think it's most the case on research.
zh: 我认为在研究方面最是这样。

speaker: Unknown
en: We have the very, very best...
zh: 我们有非常、非常最好的……

speaker: Unknown
en: ... the case on research.
zh: ……研究方面的情况。

speaker: Unknown
en: We have the very, very best researchers in the world.
zh: 我们拥有世界上非常、非常最好的研究人员。

speaker: Unknown
en: But even you look on product, we have Ami Vora, she is phenomenal.
zh: 但即使你看产品方面，我们有 Ami Vora，她是非常非凡的。

speaker: Unknown
en: We have Mike Krieger.
zh: 我们有 Mike Krieger。

speaker: Unknown
en: You're like, okay, casually started Instagram.
zh: 你会想，好吧，随便开启了 Instagram。

speaker: Unknown
en: He's here.

speaker: Unknown
en: On growth, we have John Egan, who's my engineering counterpart, who's the OG in growth engineering.
zh: 在增长方面，我们有 John Egan，他是我的工程搭档，是增长工程领域的 OG（元老）。

speaker: Unknown
en: He's great.
zh: 他很棒。

speaker: Unknown
en: We have Alexey, he's a guy who teaches growth engineering at Reforge.
zh: 我们有 Alexey，他是个在 Reforge 教授增长工程的人。

speaker: Unknown
en: He's just another dude on the team.
zh: 他只是团队里的另一个家伙。

speaker: Unknown
en: And all of that I think is just very special.
zh: 所有这些我认为都非常特别。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think my favorite here is in the [inaudible 01:27:33] a couple of months ago we had our onsite, company-wide onsite in October, and I'm walking around, I see this guy, he's just walking around eating popcorn by himself.
zh: 我认为我最喜欢的一件事是在 [听不清 01:27:33]，几个月前我们要有现场会议，全公司范围的现场会议是在十月，我在四处走动，我看到这家伙，他正独自一人四处走动吃爆米花。

speaker: Unknown
en: I go up to him and I'm like, "You're Jeff, right?"
zh: 我走向他并说，“你是 Jeff，对吧？”

speaker: Unknown
en: And he's like, "I am."
zh: 他说，“我是。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And I'm like, "You are literally the US ambassador to my country, Australia, and you are just an employee here."
zh: 我说，“你字面上就是我国澳大利亚的美国大使，而你只是这里的一名员工。”

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm talking to our prime ministers of our country and all these things.
zh: 我正在和我们要国家的总理们谈论以及所有这些事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it's just the talent combined with that culture, I think, is just this secret sauce that is the reason that I think we are as successful as we are.
zh: 而且正是结合了那种文化的这种人才，我认为，就是这种秘密配方，这就是我认为我们要如此成功的原因。

speaker: Unknown
en: This notebook channel is so interesting just as a tactical thing.
zh: 这个 notebook channel 作为一种战术性的事情非常有意思。

speaker: Unknown
en: So the idea there is Dario just shares... It's a little internal Twitter feed where folks just share what they're thinking about and what their priorities are and things like that.
zh: 所以那里的想法是 Dario 只是分享……这是一个内部的类似 Twitter 的信息流，大家只是分享他们在想什么，他们的优先事项是什么，以及类似的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, that's basically where it's an internal feed where... It's not just Dario, everyone has one.
zh: 是的，这基本上是一个内部信息流，那里……不仅仅是 Dario，每个人都有一个。

speaker: Unknown
en: And basically you share your internal thoughts.
zh: 基本上你分享你的内心想法。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's a way to keep people updated on things on what's working.
zh: 这是一种让大家更新关于什么有效的信息的方式。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's a way where people share provocative things.
zh: 这是一种人们分享挑衅性事物的方式。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think from a leadership level, and we also think about that as it's a way to scale your beliefs and views across an org as it grows quickly, right?
zh: 我认为从领导层面来看，我们也认为这是一种随着组织快速发展来扩展你的信念和观点的方式，对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: So if you're now adding a lot of people in the organization, you need to think about, "Okay, what are the behaviors that we want to model?
zh: 所以如果你现在在组织中增加很多人，你需要考虑，“好吧，我们要塑造的行为是什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: What are the principles that are top of mind to us?"
zh: 我们最在意的原则是什么？”

speaker: Unknown
en: And if you think about, yeah, you can have a bunch of these meetings where you talk about it, you can model that behavior.
zh: 如果你考虑，是的，你可以有一堆会议谈论它，你可以塑造那种行为。

speaker: Unknown
en: But if you have a channel which is like, "Here's my top of mind," and you say these things, right, if I have a post, which I did the other week of this is the importance of being comfortable leaving money on the table.
zh: 但如果你有一个像这样的频道，“这是我首先想到的，”你说这些事情，对吧，如果我有一个帖子，我几周前做过一个帖子，是关于对把钱留在桌面上感到舒适的重要性。

speaker: Unknown
en: Now all the new engineers on growth who've joined have seen that and they're like, "Oh, okay, this is different to what we've known before."
zh: 现在加入增长团队的所有新工程师都看到了那个，他们会想，“噢，好吧，这和我们以前知道的有所不同。”

speaker: Unknown
en: Right.
zh: 对。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I think it's good for the openness, but it's also good as a leader of how you can scale your views as an organization to get people more up to speed on the way that things are done, which is I think really important to avoid that drift, which when you have so many new people signing up, that drift can lead to a lot of drift in strategy and sort of perception.
zh: 所以我认为这对开放性有好处，但作为一个领导者，这也很有好处，可以作为组织扩展你的观点，让人们更快了解事情的运作方式，我认为这对于避免那种漂移非常重要，当你有这么多新人加入时，那种漂移会导致战略和观念上的很大漂移。

speaker: Unknown
en: So it just helps run a tighter ship in that way.
zh: 所以它只是有助于以那种方式更严格地管理这艘船。

speaker: Unknown
en: And more importantly, it's data for the agents everyone's got running to help them work with all these humans.
zh: 更重要的是，它是为每个人都在运行的用来帮助他们与所有这些人一起工作的智能体提供的数据。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yes, that is very, very correct.
zh: 是的，那是非常、非常正确的。

speaker: Unknown
en: It is something that goes to Claude.
zh: 它是传送到 Claude 的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: There are certain documents in onboarding where it's like the HR team has written before editing anything on this document, please check with this person because this is a document that Claude references as a key thing, right?
zh: 在入职培训中有某些文件，上面写着人力资源团队在编辑这个文件上的任何内容之前，请先与这个人核对，因为这是 Claude 引用为关键事物的文件，对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: And so more and more these types of things of, okay, how does the growth team think about this?
zh: 所以越来越多这类事情，好吧，增长团队怎么考虑这个？

speaker: Unknown
en: How does safeguards think about this?
zh: safeguards 怎么考虑这个？

speaker: Unknown
en: You're arming Claude with that context to get better and better at helping in the future.
zh: 你正在为 Claude 提供那种背景，使其在未来更好地提供帮助。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's interesting.
zh: 那很有意思。

speaker: Unknown
en: It feels like that's something that every company's going to have to start doing is just sharing their thoughts in a structured way so that all these agents that we've all got running have what they need to know.
zh: 感觉这是每家公司都开始必须做的事情，就是以结构化的方式分享他们的想法，以便我们都在运行的所有这些智能体能够知道它们需要知道的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: Another interesting side note here is just Slack.
zh: 这里另一个有趣的旁注就是 Slack。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay.
zh: 好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: So there's all this talk of SaaS tools being replaced by AI and you guys use Slack.
zh: 所以有所有这些关于 SaaS 工具被 AI 取代的谈论，而你们使用 Slack。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think there was this famous tweet of you guys still use Workday and all these tools, all these SaaS tools.
zh: 我想有你们仍然使用 Workday 和所有这些工具、所有这些 SaaS 工具的著名推文。

speaker: Unknown
en: They're all going to be vibe coded out of existence.
zh: 它们都将被 vibe coding 排除出存在。

speaker: Unknown
en: The fact that you all, at the cutting edge of what could be built with code, still use Slack and all these other tools, to me, that's a good sign that maybe SaaS companies will be outright in the future.
zh: 你们这些人，在代码所能构建的最前沿，仍然使用 Slack 和所有这些其他工具，这一事实对我来说是一个好迹象，也许 SaaS 公司在未来会变得直截了当。

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't know if you have anything there to share.
zh: 我不知道你那里有什么可以分享的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, I think it's a really complicated picture.
zh: 是的，我认为这是一幅非常复杂的图景。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's a number of things that we just build internally ourselves.
zh: 有些事情是我们自己内部构建的。

speaker: Unknown
en: As time goes on, the ability for Claude to do that better increases.
zh: 随着时间的推移，Claude 做得更好的能力会增加。

speaker: Unknown
en: And at the same time, we use Figma a ton, we use Slack a ton, we use Workday, we use a lot of these products, and I don't see that changing in the immediate future.
zh: 与此同时,我们大量使用 Figma,大量使用 Slack,使用 Workday,使用很多这些产品,而且我看不到在不久的将来这会有所改变。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so yeah, I think there's probably some truth to some of this.
zh: 所以是的,我认为其中有些话确实有些道理。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think the other parts are like it's overblown.
zh: 我认为其他部分有些是被夸大了。

speaker: Unknown
en: And many of these products, they're customers of ours.
zh: 而且这些产品中的许多都是我们的客户。

speaker: Unknown
en: We value them a lot as customers and we use their products very, very heavily, and I don't see that changing in there.
zh: 我们非常看重他们作为客户,并且我们非常非常重度地使用他们的产品,而且我看不到那里面会有所改变。

speaker: Unknown
en: And you have better things to do.
zh: 而且有更好的事情等着你去做。

speaker: Unknown
en: Who's going to spend time building Slack at a... That is not where value is going to accrue.
zh: 谁会花时间去从头构建 Slack…… 这不是价值将积累的地方。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think it also helps you see how sophisticated these things are.
zh: 我认为这也有助于你看到这些东西是多么复杂。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's been teams thinking about these problems for a long time.
zh: 有一些团队已经在思考这些问题很长时间了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Anyway, that's a whole other tangent.
zh: 无论如何,这是完全是另一个题外话了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Just coming back to the values thing, I just want to highlight something here that's really important.
zh: 回到价值观这个话题,我想强调这里一些真正重要的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: A lot of people criticize Anthropic for talking about the dangers to humanity, throwing out all these numbers about jobs going away, just creating all this fear.
zh: 很多人批评 Anthropic 谈论对人类的危险,抛出所有关于工作消失的数字,只是在制造所有的这些恐惧。

speaker: Unknown
en: And people think it's, "Oh, we're trying to raise money or we got to get people's attention or we're just trying to create headlines."
zh: 人们认为这是,"哦,我们试图筹集资金,或者我们不得不引起人们的注意,或者我们只是试图制造头条新闻。"

speaker: Unknown
en: But everything I've ever seen internally is always just this is what we believe in.
zh: 但我在内部所见过的一切总是表明,这只是我们相信的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: We want people to know what it might be coming.
zh: 我们希望人们知道可能会发生什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: Even if it isn't bad, we want people to understand, "Here's what might happen and we are trying to avoid it."
zh: 即使它并不坏,我们也希望人们理解,"这里可能会发生什么,而我们正试图避免它。"

speaker: Unknown
en: And you might say, "Why is Anthropic even building AI if it's so dangerous?"
zh: 你可能会说,"如果 AI 如此危险,为什么 Anthropic 还要构建它?"

speaker: Unknown
en: And the understanding Ben shared is just like we think it's better that we go at this and try to build it the right way versus just stay out of it and just hope that nothing bad happens.
zh: Ben 分享的理解只是,我们认为我们投身其中并尝试以正确的方式构建它,比置身事外只是祈祷不会发生坏事更好。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think three things that come to mind there.
zh: 我想到那里的三件事。

speaker: Unknown
en: First is I go back to something I said earlier, which is that we very much think about things from an exponential lens.
zh: 首先是我回到我之前说过的话,也就是我们非常是从指数视角来思考事情的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And if you are thinking about things from a linear lens, you'll see the world very, very differently, right, because you look at where we are today and you're like, "Okay, but how much better can it be in two, three years?"
zh: 如果你是从线性视角思考事情,你会非常非常不同地看待世界,对吧,因为你看看我们今天在哪里,你会说,"好吧,但在两三年内它能好多少呢?"

speaker: Unknown
en: I think if you're looking at it from an exponential lens and you understand how exponentials work, then you just realize that a lot of this stuff is actually going to be happening sooner than people think if you're looking at it from a linear lens.
zh: 我认为如果你是从指数视角来看待它并且你理解指数是如何起作用的,那么你就意识到,如果你是从线性视角来看待它,这许多东西实际上会比人们认为的更早发生。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then if you understand that there could be upsides and downsides and the range of outcomes here, I think we very much need to be talking about the downsides so we can avoid them and push towards the upsides.
zh: 然后如果你理解这里可能存在上行空间和下行空间以及一系列结果,我认为我们非常需要谈论下行空间以便我们可以避免它们并推动走向上行空间。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think most people at the company are optimists.
zh: 我想公司里的大多数人都是乐观主义者。

speaker: Unknown
en: We're very optimistic about the future.
zh: 我们对未来非常乐观。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think it's just we understand the risk is like it is not a guaranteed that we end up in a good place.
zh: 我想只是因为我们理解风险就像它并不是一个保证我们会最终处于一个好地方的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And not enough people are talking about the risks, I think, in productive ways and who have the know-how of the risks.
zh: 我想没有足够多的人以富有成效的方式谈论风险,并且拥有关于风险的专门知识。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think there's people who may talk about the risks but are not in the game.
zh: 我想有些人可能会谈论风险但并不参与其中。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so they don't actually know fully.
zh: 所以他们实际上并不完全知道。

speaker: Unknown
en: They're not surgical on what the specific risks are, but we're in the game, we understand what's happening.
zh: 他们对具体的风险是什么并不精准,但我们参与其中,我们理解正在发生什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so that's one piece.
zh: 所以那是其中一部分。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think the second is, I think, we actually believe in this stuff more strongly than we say externally.
zh: 我认为第二点是,我们实际上在内心比我们在外说的更强烈地相信这些东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: So it is just such a key part of how we think internally that sometimes we just reword your statements because it's like people might just think we're being too over the top, but internally, that's how we think, and what we're putting out is a softer version of that at times.
zh: 所以这只是我们要如何看待的内部思维如此关键的一部分,以至于有时我们只是重新措辞你的陈述,因为因为人们可能只是认为我们做得太过分了,但在内部,那就是我们思考的方式,而我们对外发布的有时是那个的温和版本。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then I think the third piece is going to what Ben said where we think a lot about driving the race to the top.
zh: 然后我认为第三点是回到 Ben 所说的,我们非常思考关于推动向上竞争。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so it's like if you're not in the game and you're shouting from the sidelines, no one cares.
zh: 所以就像如果你不参与其中并且你在场边大喊大叫,没人在乎。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's just the reality of how the world works.
zh: 这只是世界如何运作的现实。

speaker: Unknown
en: No one really cares.
zh: 没有人真的在乎。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you're in the game and you're a leading player and what you're doing is working, you can influence people who are also in the game to take the right actions.
zh: 如果你参与其中并且你是一个领先的参与者并且你正在做的事情是有效的,你可以影响也参与其中的人采取正确的行动。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so that is the core of it.
zh: 所以这就是核心。

speaker: Unknown
en: If we just pack up and go home, you have no influence on this thing.
zh: 如果我们只是收拾行李回家,你对这件事没有任何影响力。

speaker: Unknown
en: And if you stay in the game and you're like, commercially, you're doing great, and then you have ways to influence that, okay, these are the principles and put that into the conversation and make more people sort of believe in those things.
zh: 如果你留在游戏中并且你像,在商业上,你做得很好,然后你有方法去影响它,好的,这些是原则并把它放入对话中并让更多人相信这些东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: I could talk to you forever, Amol, but two more questions just to round out the conversation and touch on some stuff that I've hinted at a number of times.
zh: 我可以和你永远聊下去,Amol,但还有两个问题只是为了结束对话并触及一些我已经暗示了很多次的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: One is I want to take us actually to failure corner because someone listening to this may be like, "All right, look at this guy just worked at all these amazing companies, cold email, the CPO at Anthropic, got a job, joined this rocket ship.
zh: 一个是我想带我们去失败角,因为听这个的人可能会像,"好吧,看这家伙只是在所有这些惊人的公司工作过,冷邮件,Anthropic 的 CPO,得到一份工作,加入这艘火箭飞船。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's all been up and to the right, just killing it constantly."
zh: 一直都是向上和向右,一直在不断获胜。"

speaker: Unknown
en: What's a story from your career where things didn't work out when you failed and what did you learn from that experience?
zh: 你的职业生涯中有一个故事是事情没有成功当你失败并且你从那个经历中学到了什么?

speaker: Unknown
en: I have a couple.
zh: 我有几个。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think it's very much not that way.
zh: 我认为完全不是那样的。

speaker: Unknown
en: I feel like it was a lot of squiggly lines to get here.
zh: 我觉得到达这里是很多弯弯曲曲的线条。

speaker: Unknown
en: The biggest I'd say is just founding a company, raising a bunch of money, having employees, and then having to shut it down and tell your investors that you've lost the money and that you had a vision of what you're going to do and that it's not going to happen.
zh: 我认为最大的一点就是创办一家公司,筹集了一大笔钱,拥有员工,然后不得不关闭它并告诉你的投资者你亏了钱,并且你对你要做的事情有一个愿景,而这不会发生了。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so that I think is probably the biggest one.
zh: 所以我想这可能是最大的一件事。

speaker: Unknown
en: We spent three years on it.
zh: 我们在这上面花了三年时间。

speaker: Unknown
en: It was not like a, "Oh, we tried something part-time."
zh: 这不像是一个，“哦，我们尝试了一些兼职的事情。”

speaker: Unknown
en: It was like, "No, we went for it."
zh: 这就像是，“不，我们全力以赴了。”

speaker: Unknown
en: We had spent three years, raised a couple of mil.
zh: 我们花了三年时间，筹集了几百万。

speaker: Unknown
en: We had maybe seven, 10 employees at our biggest.
zh: 在我们规模最大的时候，我们可能有七、十名员工。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it was something that we really believed in.
zh: 这是我们真正坚信的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: It was around mental health and how you can quantify mental health to help understand or get early predictors of things like generalized anxiety, major depression.
zh: 它是关于心理健康，以及你如何量化心理健康来帮助理解或获得诸如广泛性焦虑、重度抑郁症等事物的早期预测指标。

speaker: Unknown
en: So it's stuff that we really, really believed in.
zh: 所以这是我们真正、真正相信的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: But ultimately, in our early 20s, we had no idea what we're doing at the time and many things I do differently, but that was just a very, very, very painful process.
zh: 但最终，在我们 20 岁出头的时候，我们当时根本不知道我们在做什么，虽然现在很多事情我会做得不一样，但这那真是一个非常、非常、非常痛苦的过程。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think the good thing was we kept our investors in the loop the whole time, so 20 founders who are struggling out there.
zh: 我认为好的方面是我们一直让投资者保持知情，所以外面有 20 位正在苦苦挣扎的创始人。

speaker: Unknown
en: Send those monthly investor updates.
zh: 发送那些月度投资者更新。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's really easy to send those when things are great.
zh: 当事情顺利时，发送这些真的很容易。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's really hard to send those when things are bad.
zh: 当事情糟糕时，发送这些真的很难。

speaker: Unknown
en: You just bat it down.
zh: 你只是把它压下去。

speaker: Unknown
en: You sit down and send an update to your investors about why everything you talked about last month did not go to plan, but it's just the right thing to do, and it keeps people in the loop, and it avoids surprises.
zh: 你坐下来给你的投资者发一份关于为什么你上个月谈论的一切都没有按计划进行的更新，但这是正确的做法，它让人们保持知情，并避免意外。

speaker: Unknown
en: But it's still so tough when you're then calling up the investors to be like, "Hey, we're shutting down or making that decision," everyone who believed in you and it becomes such a big part of the identity.
zh: 但当你随后给投资者打电话说，“嘿，我们要关闭了或做出那个决定”时，仍然非常艰难，每一个相信你的人，而且它成为了身份认同的很大一部分。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's very, very tough.
zh: 这非常、非常艰难。

speaker: Unknown
en: So man, that was just brutal.
zh: 所以伙计，那真是太残酷了。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's brutal.
zh: 这很残酷。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it took me, I think, a number of years to truly get over.
zh: 我想我花了几年才真正从中走出来。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think that it's a tough thing, but you get so much from that experience that it's hard to see in the moment.
zh: 我认为这是一件艰难的事情，但你从那次经历中获得了很多东西，这在当下很难看到。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think without doing that, I would not have gone into this.
zh: 我认为如果没有做那件事，我就不会进入这个领域。

speaker: Unknown
en: I was not a PM before.
zh: 我之前不是 PM（产品经理）。

speaker: Unknown
en: I didn't have any of these skills.
zh: 我没有任何这些技能。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'd never worked on products.
zh: 我从来没有做过产品。

speaker: Unknown
en: I didn't know how to cold email really.
zh: 我真的不知道如何写冷邮件。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so it was through that job that I learned a ton of the skills that made this career path viable for me.
zh: 所以正是通过那份工作，我学到了大量的技能，使得这条职业路径对我来说成为可能。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it's just really hard to see that in the moment when you're looking at a point in time.
zh: 当你着眼于某个时间点时，真的很难看到这一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's much easier to draw the line looking back.
zh: 回过头来看，划出这条线就容易多了。

speaker: Unknown
en: But I think that'd be my takeaway is just keep in mind that it's a long game and some of those things... I'm so grateful for that experience now.
zh: 我想这就是我的收获，就是请记住这是一场长线游戏，其中一些事情……我现在真的很感激那次经历。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm so grateful that it failed and went that way, actually.
zh: 我真的很感激它失败了并走了那条路，实际上。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's very painful because it wouldn't have led to what I'm doing now.
zh: 这很痛苦，因为它不会导致我现在做的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so it's a tough thing, but the positives can come from it.
zh: 所以这是一件艰难的事情，但积极的东西可以从中产生。

speaker: Unknown
en: What I love about this is a lot of people have these times in their career where they're like, "It's all over.
zh: 我喜欢这一点的是，很多人在他们的职业生涯中有这样的时刻，他们会觉得，“一切都结束了。

speaker: Unknown
en: I failed in such a big way and my reputation's screwed and people were counting on me and now they see I'm an imposter.
zh: 我失败得好惨，我的名声毁了，人们指望我，现在他们看到我是个冒名顶替者。

speaker: Unknown
en: I never knew what I was doing.
zh: 我从来不知道我在做什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: And after all, now they finally see."
zh: 毕竟，现在他们终于看到了。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And just seeing that, and this was three years, I'm looking at your LinkedIn, you had seven employees, something like that, and then it's like masterclass, Mercury, Anthropic.
zh: 看到这一点，而且这是三年，我正在看你的 LinkedIn，你有七名员工，诸如此类，然后就像是 masterclass, Mercury, Anthropic。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think it's inspiring to hear a story like that to know that you can have a big failure like that and things can work out.
zh: 听到这样的故事很受鼓舞，要知道你可以经历那样大的失败，但事情仍然会有好的结果。

speaker: Unknown
en: That is not the end.
zh: 那不是终点。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay.
zh: 好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: So at 2023, I was going to go on pat leave because my wife's pregnant and I was just like, "Wait, what do I do with the newsletter?
zh: 所以在 2023 年，我要去休陪产假，因为我的妻子怀孕了，我当时就想，“等等，我该怎么处理这个时事通讯？

speaker: Unknown
en: I need to take some time off.
zh: 我需要休息一段时间。

speaker: Unknown
en: That would be really nice to take a month or two off."
zh: 休息一两个月真的很好。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And so my plan was, "Okay, I'm going to do a bunch of guest posts where people line up."
zh: 所以我的计划是，“好吧，我要做一系列的客座文章，让人们排队。”

speaker: Unknown
en: I put out a call for guest posts, people apply, I pick a few and then I kind of slot them in ahead of time so I could take time off.
zh: 我发出了客座文章的征集，人们申请，我挑选了几个，然后我提前把他们排进去，这样我就可以休息了。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I put out this call, "Hey, who wants to write a guest post for my newsletter?"
zh: 所以我发出了这个征集，“嘿，谁想为我的时事通讯写一篇客座文章？”

speaker: Unknown
en: I got 500 plus applications.
zh: 我收到了 500 多份申请。

speaker: Unknown
en: One of those applications was from you and the pitch was essentially how a traumatic brain injury made me a better product manager.
zh: 其中一份申请是来自你的，那个推介本质上就是脑外伤如何让我成为更好的产品经理。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I still remember reading the first draft you sent me and I was just like, "Holy shit, this story is incredible."
zh: 我还记得读你发给我的初稿，我当时就想，“天哪，这个故事太不可思议了。”

speaker: Unknown
en: It's just like you feel such feelings and it was just so tactically interesting and useful like, "Oh wow, this is actually going to make me a better product manager."
zh: 就像你会感觉到这种情感，而且它在战术上是如此有趣和有用，就像，“噢哇，这真的会让我成为一个更好的产品经理。”

speaker: Unknown
en: Share the story of the brain injury that you went through and just the journey that you went on there because this is going to blow people's minds.
zh: 分享一下你经历的脑损伤故事，以及你在那里的旅程，因为这会让人们大吃一惊。

speaker: Unknown
en: It was the toughest time in my life.
zh: 那是我生命中最艰难的时期。

speaker: Unknown
en: It made shutting down a company being like, "Oh, that was nothing actually."
zh: 这让关闭一家公司看起来就像是，“噢，那其实不算什么。”

speaker: Unknown
en: Just funny how perspective works that way.
zh: 视角的运作方式就是如此有趣。

speaker: Unknown
en: Back in early 2022, I had a traumatic brain injury.
zh: 回到 2022 年初，我遭受了创伤性脑损伤。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I'd done MMA for many years.
zh: 我练习 MMA（综合格斗）很多年了。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'd done Muay Thai, which is a type of martial arts for many years, never had a problem.
zh: 我练习泰拳，一种武术很多年了，从来没出过问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it's just one of the things that happens, the wrong session.
zh: 这只是会发生的事情之一，错误的训练时段。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's just a normal day of sparring, nothing crazy.
zh: 这只是一个普通的实战日，没什么疯狂的。

speaker: Unknown
en: You get the wrong hit to the head in the wrong way, and my whole life changed.
zh: 你的头部受到错误的撞击，我的整个人生都改变了。

speaker: Unknown
en: And basically I spent nine months.
zh: 基本上我花了九个月的时间。

speaker: Unknown
en: I was off work for nine months.
zh: 我休假了九个月。

speaker: Unknown
en: The first couple of months were brutal.
zh: 前几个月非常残酷。

speaker: Unknown
en: It took me roughly half a year till I was comfortable walking again.
zh: 大概花了半年时间我才又能舒适地走路。

speaker: Unknown
en: It was very, very difficult.
zh: 非常非常困难。

speaker: Unknown
en: The first two, three months beyond just showering and going to the bathroom, my wife did everything for me, including texting my friends.
zh: 在前两三个月里，除了洗澡和上厕所，我妻子为我做了一切，包括替我给朋友发短信。

speaker: Unknown
en: I would listen to music for maybe 20 seconds and feel like I needed to vomit.
zh: 我听音乐大概只能听 20 秒，就觉得我想吐。

speaker: Unknown
en: I couldn't look at screens at all.
zh: 我根本无法看屏幕。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it was a very, very long recovery.
zh: 那是一段非常非常漫长的恢复期。

speaker: Unknown
en: It was not clear to me that I would ever work again for a while actually.
zh: 在很长一段时间里，我不清楚我是否还能再工作。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it was like we'd even discussed my wife, what would we do in that case, et cetera.
zh: 我们甚至和我妻子讨论过，那种情况下我们会怎么做等等。

speaker: Unknown
en: And we had to think even on those levels.
zh: 我们甚至不得不考虑那个层面的问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: And through a lot of pushing and really working through myself, and you have to just slowly increase your tolerance to things.
zh: 通过大量的努力和真正的自我磨练，你必须慢慢提高对事物的耐受性。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's a brutal process, but you need to slowly expose yourself to different things and just get better and better at each little thing, actively work on that, don't push it too far, otherwise you have a big setback.
zh: 这是一个残酷的过程，但你需要慢慢接触不同的事物，并且在每件小事上做得越来越好，主动去练习，不要用力过猛，否则你会遭遇重大挫折。

speaker: Unknown
en: It basically got to the point then where over nine months and then that it returned to work and then it slowly got better.
zh: 基本上到了那个地步，经过九个月，然后重返工作岗位，然后慢慢好转。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the part, Lenny, you don't know is in mid-2023, we posted that newsletter and a month later I got re-injured actually.
zh: Lenny，你不知道的部分是，在 2023 年年中，我们发布了那篇通讯，一个月后我又受伤了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Oh, wow.
zh: 噢，哇。

speaker: Unknown
en: And when you have a brain injury, until you're 100% healed, your risk of another concussion or a brain injury is elevated.
zh: 当你患有脑损伤时，在 100% 康复之前，你再次遭受脑震荡或脑损伤的风险是升高的。

speaker: Unknown
en: When you're 100% healed, your risk falls down to that of a non-concussed population.
zh: 当你 100% 康复时，你的风险会降到未受过脑震荡人群的水平。

speaker: Unknown
en: But until that time, and even if you're like 95% healed, you're not 100% healed.
zh: 但直到那时，即使你 95% 康复了，你也没有 100% 康复。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it was just an innocuous sort of in everyday life getting off a plane, a bag sort of hit me on the head type thing.
zh: 那只是日常生活中的一次无辜事件，下飞机时，一个包撞到了我的头那一类的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I was off work for two months when I... One month into joining Mercury, one month in, I'm like, "Sorry guys, I need to peace out for three months."
zh: 我休假了两个月，那时我……加入 Mercury 一个月后，刚进去一个月，我就说，“抱歉伙计们，我需要撤出去三个月。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And then it was a very, very long recovery from that.
zh: 然后从那之后的恢复非常非常漫长。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I'm actually still not 100% healed.
zh: 而且我实际上还没有 100% 康复。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I'm mostly good, but I have times when I have dizziness and headaches and other things that I need to work around.
zh: 所以我基本上还好，但我有时会有头晕和头痛以及我需要应对的其他问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: Overall, I feel like it's one of the best things that could have happened to me, and I think that keeping that mindset helps.
zh: 总的来说，我觉得这是可能发生在我身上最好的事情之一，我认为保持这种心态很有帮助。

speaker: Unknown
en: And there's a point when you can take that too far when that view is detached from reality, but I think it's just made me so much better and more effective as a person.
zh: 当这种观点脱离现实时，你可能会有点过头，但我认为这真的让我作为一个变得更更好、更有效率了。

speaker: Unknown
en: A lot of the same habits, I don't drink alcohol, I don't drink caffeine, I have to do a bunch of these things for my physical health.
zh: 很多同样的习惯，我不喝酒，不喝咖啡，我必须为了身体健康做这一堆事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: I just have to do them.
zh: 我只是不得不做这些。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I keep doing them.
zh: 所以我一直在做。

speaker: Unknown
en: I take breaks.
zh: 我会休息。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is a really big one.
zh: 这是一个非常重要的一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: You might think even in a place like Anthropic, how do you survive in this way?
zh: 你可能甚至会觉得在像 Anthropic 这样的地方，你怎么能以这种方式生存？

speaker: Unknown
en: It's like even on the craziest days, Lenny, between the start of the day and lunch and between lunch and the end of the day, I take a short break.
zh: 就像即使在最疯狂的日子里，Lenny，在一天开始到午餐之间，以及午餐到一天结束之间，我会短暂休息一下。

speaker: Unknown
en: Even on the craziest days with model launches, et cetera, I'm lucky we have a meditation area in the office that I'll go to.
zh: 即使在有 model 发布等等的最疯狂的日子里，我很幸运我们在办公室里有一个冥想区，我会去那里。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then the whole side of things around meditation that I talked about in that post, I think both you and I have done a retreat at Spirit Rock and doing a meditation retreat changed my life.
zh: 还有关于我在那篇文章中谈到的冥念那一整面，我想你我都去过 Spirit Rock 的静修，参加一次冥想静修改变了我的生活。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it's something that I do at least once a year now.
zh: 这是我现在每年至少做一次的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: I have one coming up relatively soon as well.
zh: 我很快也有一个即将到来。

speaker: Unknown
en: And just I think all of that has helped with better managing the physical side of it because this job is very taxing, and then emotionally having a little bit of space to what happens.
zh: 我只是觉得所有这些都有助于更好地管理身体方面，因为这项工作是非常累人的，然后在情感上，对于发生的事情有一点空间。

speaker: Unknown
en: So you have sort of awareness on one side, you have the reality on the other side.
zh: 所以你在一边有一种觉知，你在另一边有现实。

speaker: Unknown
en: And reality's crazy here, man.
zh: 而这里的现实很疯狂，伙计。

speaker: Unknown
en: Reality's insane.
zh: 现实简直难以置信。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's so much happening every day.
zh: 每天都有这么多事情发生。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's so much noise, it's mind-blowing.
zh: 有这么多噪音，这简直让人难以置信。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so that relationship between awareness and reality, that's where you have choice that you sort of learn from deep meditation on how to shift that and apply your choice there.
zh: 所以觉知和现实之间的这种关系，那是你拥有选择的地方，你从深度冥想中学会了如何转移它并在那里应用你的选择。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think that that is the thing that has helped me significantly with just keeping a more level head.
zh: 我认为那是对我帮助最大的事情，让我能保持更冷静的头脑。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think a lot of staying in this game in this level of intensity is just keep your head, just don't lose your head in the crazy times.
